vivo | Yes | For dedicated sensing RS, sensing-oriented BM is needed. For “reuse communication RS and enhanced communication RS” for sensing but not used for communication, sensing-oriented BM is needed. For “reuse communication RS and enhanced communication RS” for sensing and also is used for communication, joint communication and sensing-oriented BM is needed.
SONY | Yes | 
Xiaomi | [yes] | Yes, in principle. Some clarification may be needed: For “beam indication”, what is the difference compared to TCI states? For example, is this intended to support RX beam indication from UE to aNB? What is meant with “Beam maintenance” in the context of sensing? In communication, we usually mean that the UE “maintains” its best RX beam for given TX beams (best TX/RX beam pair). The UE measures continuously SSB and maintains its best RX beams for each SSB. Then, when it gets a TCI state indicated for a data transmission, it can pick the best RX beam according to the QCL:ed SSB. However, for sensing, this is different. At least for initial detection. There, the UE probably has to sweep its RX beams for the same TX beam (similar to P3 stage), the scan a large angular area.
Qualcomm | No | We consider this as low priority; As a first thought, communication-based beam management are applicable to sensing, and no specific handling may be needed for sensing. We acknowledge that there could be specific enhancenemtns one could envision, but given the current progress, and the open items that exist, we consider this as lower priority.

LGE | Yes | We support studying sensing-oriented beam management, considering differences from communication. In particular, beam alignment should consider the target sensing area (TSA), where sensing Tx/Rx beams are directed toward the TSA rather than communication-centric directions. In addition, beam design and selection should consider path-level observability, i.e., the ability to resolve and distinguish target-related paths from non-target/background paths.

Interference handling

Summary on company views

Study the relevant scenarios for interference handling: Xiaomi, ZTE, SS, Apple, E///, CMCC, Google, DCM, LG, NEC, Cohere, Transsion, NIST, ETRI, CU, CAICT

Between sensing and communication: SS, DCM, CATT, CMCC, LGE, ETRI, ZTE

To avoid 3dB increase of noise floor, communication within 5km should be muted: CMCC

Between sensing signals: SS, DCM, CATT, LGE, ETRI, ZTE

Increased LOS interference between cells for low-altitude use case: CATT

BS-BS interference: ZTE

UE-UE interference: ZTE

Self-interference for monostatic sensing mode

Up to implemenation: DCM

Large antenna isolation: ZTE

LFM: ZTE

Pulse: ZTE

Interference handling solutions: Apple, ETRI, OPPO, IDC, SS, CATT, ZTE, Huawei

Coordinated resource allocation: Google

UL resource muting: ETRI, ZTE, CATT, Jio, LGE, CT, IDCC

PDSCH rate matching around sensing RS resources: E//, Jio, CT

Transparent to communicaiton UE or sensing-incapable UE: OPPO

“none” type resources in AI 10.8.3: E//

Spatial domain interference mitigation: ZTE, Google

Power domain interference mitigation: ZTE

Multiplexing sensing and communication: ZTE, DCM

CSI/Interference measurement based on sensing RS: ZTE

inform non-sensing UE the sensing RS configuration for interference measurement: ZTE

unified CSI framework: ZTE

transmitter-side interference avoidance and interference coordination: CATT

receiver-side interference cancellation: CATT

CATT: the assistance information to be reported needs further study

Inter-cell TRS interference mitigation: QC

Interference measurement and report

Interference measurement resource: HW, CATT

HW: RSRP/RSRQ/SINR defined in the interested domain for sensing, e.g. delay-doppler domain, delay-angle domain, etc.

Interference measurement: HW, CATT, ETRI

finer-grained path-level interference assessment: HW

same ambiguity range in the interested domain for the sensing measurement: HW

Interference measurement report: HW, CATT

E//: Do not support additional reporting mechanisms for the purpose of interference measurement and report

[Moderator’s note] based on the contributions, it is common view that certain interference handling between sensing signals and between sensing and communication is an important topic for study. Various solutions are also general discussed by different companies, including the interference mitigation/coordination in time/frequency domain, spatial domain or power domain, etc. Please comment on the following proposal.

CATT, CICTCI | Yes | It is necessary to further investigate interference mitigation schemes, including transmitter-side avoidance, interference coordination, and receiver-side cancellation. Regarding receiver-side cancellation in particular, the required assistance information (e.g., interfering RS parameters provided to the receiver) warrants further study.
IDCC | Yes | Resource reservation may unnecessarily bias the study toward static or semi-static interference mitigation approaches. Further study should preserve flexibility for adaptive and dynamic coordinated resource allocation mechanisms for ISAC. Therefore, we can go a more general proposal that might be more agreeable and helpful for guiding the study Study the interference for sensing and identify the difference if any between interference for sensing and for communication for aNB-UE and UE-aNB bistatic sensing, including the interference definition, interference measurement resources and interference measurement reports, etc.

Power control

Summary on company views

Study power control for sensing: DCM, CATT, OPPO, Apple, NEC, LGE, ZTE, Nokia, vivo, SPRD, Cohere, IDCC, CAICT, Google

NO: HW

Consider both sensing performance and impacts to communication: DCM, vivo, Xiaomi

Two ISAC approaches of communication/sensing common signals/resources and sensing dedicated signals/resources: DCM

Constant power in a CPI: OPPO, Apple, E//, Xiaomi

Power control for shared RS for communication and sensing: LGE

power boosting of the sensing RS: LGE

collaborative design for power-related procedures between sensing and communication: NEC

UL PC: NokiaSPRD, vivo, OPPO, ZTE, CAICT, Xiaomi

P0/alpha: Xiaomi, E//, Apple, IDCC

Separate OL PC on sensing RS aiming at reference path and target path: E//

Pathloss reference

pathloss of STX-target-SRX path: CATT, LGE, NEC, ZTE, Xiaomi, Apple, vivo, NEC

control sensing signal Tx power based on the distance between the TSA and the sensing Tx and/or Rx: LGE

sensing reference entity: Apple

Apple: Multiple SRX experiences different coupling loss to the target, requiring multiple power reference points

OL vs. CL

OL PC as starting point: E//

At least OL PC: CATT

CL PC: Xiaomi, Apple, CAICT, IDCC, CATT

one or more sensing power control parameter sets (P0, alpha, closed-loop index): Apple

Sensing PHR procedure: NEC, Apple, IDCC

power ramping mechanisms: Apple

CATT: two solutions independent or dependent on DL RS measurement

DL PC: Nokia, ZTE, IDCC

Downlink power allocation for 5G communication can be used as a starting point: CATT

Dedicated power offset on Sensing RS, which is independent with that of communication: ZTE

[Moderator’s note] More companies propose to study power control for both UL sensing and DL sensing. Sensing performance and impact to communication are two main key consideration points. A key observation is that sensing performance is determined by the target path, but not the full received power arrived at UE, which may make it different from communication. As discussed by Apple, due to the broadcast nature of sensing RS, the Tx power control should consider the reception of multiple SRX. Some companies propose constant power in a CPI which is actually related to phase continuity in section 6.4.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1

On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication.

PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC)

P0/alpha

Pathloss reference

CL PC

PHR

Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | Regarding UL PC, it is necessary to study how to compensate the pathloss of STX-target-SRX path, rather than STX-SRX, At least open-loop power control should be supported. For DL power allocation, we remain open to further discussion. The Sensing RS power offset could be configured either independently of or dependent on the communication signals.
ZTE |  | Again, we prefer to clarify this is for sensing RS, rather than data. We don’t think additional PC is needed in the case data is an sensing signal. On power control for sensing RS, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)
IDCC | Yes | The current proposal explicitly mentions CL PC under UL power control aspects, which may implicitly narrow the study scope toward closed-loop-only approaches. However, sensing operation may involve diverse deployment scenarios, sensing latency constraints, mobility conditions, and signaling overhead considerations where open-loop power control (OL PC) mechanisms may also be beneficial. Therefore, the study should preserve flexibility to evaluate both open-loop and closed-loop power control approaches for sensing signals from UE. Hence, we propose the following modifications: [FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1 On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC OL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)
vivo | Yes | For SRS for both sensing and communication, joint PC is needed. For SRS only used for sensing, sensing-oriented PC is needed.
Xiaomi | Yes | 
Qualcomm | No | We consider this as low priority; As a first thought, communication-based power control are applicable to sensing, and no specific handling may be needed for sensing. We acknowledge that there could be specific enhancements one could envision, but given the current progress, and the open items that exist, we consider this as lower priority.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Same proposal as the last meeting and we made the comments already in the last meeting. We also studied this issue after the last meeting but we still suspect the necessity for the specific power control handling for sensing. The reason is the communication and sensing share the same PA hardware, simply adjusting the PA to satisfy the STx-ST-SRx path may not be feasible at all if the PA has maximized the transmit power to satisfy the STx-SRx path for communication. On the other hand, if sensing is to assist comm, it may even not be expected to have different transmit power from that for comm. Again, such details are not helpful for guiding the study. Instead, the more general proposal can go like Study the necessity of specific power control for sensing for aNB-UE bistatic sensing compared to the power control mechanism for communication.
Nokia | No | At first we should study under what conditions that sensing PC is needed. Without clear justifications, it’s hard to design these PC parameters.
MediaTek | NO | It’s a little bit early to discuss the power control related aspects since there is not any details design on sensing RS/resource allocation aspects, it can be discussed at WI phase.
LGE |  | We support studying sensing-specific power control mechanisms. Power control should consider target-path characteristics rather than conventional link-based metrics, and balance sensing performance with communication impact. In addition, we suggest to study power boosting if sensing RS is RE-level FDMed with communication channel/signal to enhance detectability of weak target reflections. [FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1 On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation) Power boosting for sensing RS
Apple | Yes | There is a need to add a dynamic power control or power ramping method similar to PRACH.
NEC | Yes | PC and PHR is important from PHY procedure to ensure a high-quality sensing.
Samsung |  | We are generally fine with this proposal. In our view, PC for sensing is to determine the appropriate power for target detection/tracking while the PC for communication is to maintain the reliable UE-TRP communication link. Considering the different purposes, we think it is reasonable to study OL/CL PC at least for UL sensing.
PCL |  | We see the value of this topic. However, detailed power control parameters might be premature until the foundational aspects (RS design, beam management, evaluation methodology) are more mature. Our suggestion would be to defer this to a later stage (e.g., RAN1 #126bis or #127) to allow for a more productive discussion.
Moderator |  | Considering inputs from QC, HW, MTK, Nokia, I add “necessity” in the proposal. So, please interested company clarify the motivation and solutions when you discuss PC in future discussions.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Same proposal as the last meeting and we made the comments already in the last meeting. We also studied this issue after the last meeting but we still suspect the necessity for the specific power control handling for sensing. The reason is the communication and sensing share the same PA hardware, simply adjusting the PA to satisfy the STx-ST-SRx path may not be feasible at all if the PA has maximized the transmit power to satisfy the STx-SRx path for communication. On the other hand, if sensing is to assist comm, it may even not be expected to have different transmit power from that for comm. Again, such details are not helpful for guiding the study. Instead, the more general proposal can go like Study the necessity of specific power control for sensing for aNB-UE bistatic sensing compared to the power control mechanism for communication.

IDCC | Comment | We agree that P0/alpha, pathloss reference, and the recently added power ramping are all related to open-loop power control. However, open-loop aspects are not necessarily limited to only these parameters, e.g., ΔTF (or equivalent transmission-format-related offset): Offset depending on bandwidth, MCS, modulation, number of RBs, etc. Pmax (or PHR removed in the new proposal) Similarly, for closed-loop power control, TPC commands are the primary mechanism, but closed-loop operation may also involve additional related parameters and procedures, e.g., TPC accumulation state (Whether TPC commands are accumulated or absolute) TPC step size (Step of power adjustment (e.g., ±1 dB, ±2 dB, etc.)) reference measurement/feedback metric Therefore, we prefer to keep the higher-level categories “OL PC” and “CL PC,” while listing P0/alpha, pathloss reference, power ramping, and TPC commands as example parameters/aspects under each category. If above is not agreeable, then at least include a note that other power control related parameters are not precluded. | We agree that P0/alpha, pathloss reference, and the recently added power ramping are all related to open-loop power control. However, open-loop aspects are not necessarily limited to only these parameters, e.g., ΔTF (or equivalent transmission-format-related offset): Offset depending on bandwidth, MCS, modulation, number of RBs, etc. Pmax (or PHR removed in the new proposal) Similarly, for closed-loop power control, TPC commands are the primary mechanism, but closed-loop operation may also involve additional related parameters and procedures, e.g., TPC accumulation state (Whether TPC commands are accumulated or absolute) TPC step size (Step of power adjustment (e.g., ±1 dB, ±2 dB, etc.)) reference measurement/feedback metric Therefore, we prefer to keep the higher-level categories “OL PC” and “CL PC,” while listing P0/alpha, pathloss reference, power ramping, and TPC commands as example parameters/aspects under each category. If above is not agreeable, then at least include a note that other power control related parameters are not precluded.

## R1-2603996 - R1-2603996_Moderator (Xiaomi)_Summary #3 on integration with communication for ISAC.docx
Source: E:\标准相关资料\RAN1_125_ISAC_文档整理_20260616\RAN1_125_ISAC\02_Integration_with_Communication\Moderator_Summaries\R1-2603996_Moderator (Xiaomi)_Summary #3 on integration with communication for ISAC.docx

3GPP TSG RAN WG1 #125 R1-2603996

Dalian, CN, May 18th ~ 22nd, 2026

Agenda item: 10.8.2

Source: Moderator (Xiaomi)

Title: Summary #3 on integration with communication for ISAC

Document for: Discussion/Decision

Introduction

In RAN #108, the SID on Study on 6G radio was agreed with the following objectives for ISAC [1].

[TABLE]
Sensing – Studies to be based on use cases and associated requirements, as defined in [TR38.914] PHY functions and procedures for sensing technology (e.g., waveform. reference signals, measurement feedback, etc…) [RAN1, RAN4] Evaluate sensing performance and if necessary, extend channel modelling, for the selected use cases [RAN1] Aspects of integration with communication services [RAN1] higher layer procedures and protocol aspects [RAN2] RAN4 aspects of sensing including RF, coexistence, and testability in coordination with other WGs [RAN4] Note: RAN1 identify detailed requirements, if triggered by TSG RAN

This document summarizes the contributions and discussions on the aspects of integration with communication for 6G ISAC in RAN1 #125 meeting.

The proposals in this document are tagged and color coded respectively

[H] Proposal: High Priority, to be handled firstly.

[M] Proposal: Medium Priority, we will handle such proposals on best effort basis.

The new/revised proposals in current round for discussion is further tagged with [FL3]. Note: Some proposals without update are still marked with early round number. If any company didn’t provide a comment yet, or prefer to update the comments, feel free to continue comment on them.

The following email thread is assigned for discussion of the study item (agenda 10.8.2):

[125-R20-6GR-ISAC-Integration] Email discussion on Rel-20 6GR-ISAC-integration–Yingyang (Xiaomi)

To be used for sharing updates on online/offline schedule, details on what is to be discussed in online/offline sessions, tdoc number of the moderator summary for online session, etc

Discussion plan

In this meeting, let’s still prioritize the clarification on the scope of 6GR ISAC study. The following priority order of the proposals is proposed

H proposals in section 6

H proposals in section 4

H proposals in section 5

M proposals if stable

[FL3][M] Proposal 4.1-1-rev3

The following options can be considered to generate the RE pattern of sensing RS

Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS, DMRS, PTRS, SSB

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS, DMRS, PTRS, SSB

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

Option 3: Additional comb factor, e.g., 1,2,3,6 for CSI-RS in addition to existing comb factor 4, 12, 24.

Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols

Option 5: Non-uniform subcarrier pattern within an OFDM symbol

Option 6: Time-staggered subcarrier mapping across symbols of communication RS

Other options are not precluded

Combination of multiple options are not precluded

NO

E//: Sensing RS configuration is decoupled from sensing measurement configuration

OPPO: DL or UL sensing signal should be categorized from transmitter perspective

LGE: CPI duration may be constrained based on the range resolution and the maximum expected target velocity

CMCC: Study the metrics, which are simple to evaluated and to compared among companies, for the evaluation of sensing RS performance. The following metrics can be considered:

Range estimation accuracy

Unambiguous Range

Doppler estimation accuracy

Unambiguous Doppler

[Moderator’s note] Based on all inputs, it is well aligned that RAN1 should start sensing RS design with reusing communication RS. The pros/cons of using each individual type of communication RS for sensing are discussed by multiple companies. In short, due to the low density in time/frequency of communication RS, a single communication RS cannot meet some KPIs in multiple use cases requiring large coverage and velocity range, e.g., UAV and vehicle.

Enhancements are expected taking reuse of communication RS as staring point. There are in general two solutions for enhancements. One is to combine multiple communication RS of same or different RS types. The other one is to directly change some parameters of the communication RS, e.g., comb factor and/or the time periodicity. The two solutions can effectively generate the same RE pattern for sensing RS. Further, some companies prefer to keep uniform RE allocation in frequency but allow non-uniform OFDM symbol allocation in time.

From the moderator’s understanding, the exact solution to configure/indicate a sensing RS is/can be a WI issue. In the current time being, RAN1 can focus on the principles to design the RE patterns of sensing RS and related evaluations. For example, whether non-uniform RE pattern can provide resource overhead gain with marginal or reasonable sensing performance degradation. Evaluation by LLS or SLS on the RE patterns is expected.

[FL1][M] Proposal 4.1-1

The following options can be considered to generate sensing RS based on communication RS

Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

Option 2: One or multiple communication RS of multiple RS types are combined as a sensing RS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

Option 3: Introduce comb factor {1,2,3,6} for CSI-RS in addition to {4, 12, 24}

Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols

Other options are not precluded

Combination of multiple options are not precluded

FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS

Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | We support both Option 1 and Option 2. Enhanced RS for sensing should be supported to facilitate diverse sensing patterns. For instance, multiple resources—whether of the same or different RS types—could be aggregated to construct denser uniform or non-uniform sensing signal patterns.
ZTE |  | In last meeting, we agreed three ways for sensing RS. From our view, option 1 and 2 are method 1. Option 3 and option 4 are communication RS enhancement, i.e. method 2. We prefer to clarify that. For option 1 and 2, we prefer make DMRS and PTRS in the same level as CSI-RS, SRS, anyway all of them should be studied. In addition, TRS is a part of CSI-RS, and not agreed in 6GR yet. We prefer to make those RS just examples. In the last note, all RS should be up to aNB implementation rather than SF. The following options can be studied to resue communication RS for sensing Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS E.g. the possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS, DMRS, PTRS Option 2: One or multiple communication RS of multiple RS types are combined as a sensing RS E.g., the possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS, DMRS, PTRS The following options can be studied to enhance communication RS for sensingOption 3: Introduce comb factor {1,2,3,6} for CSI-RS in addition to {4, 12, 24} Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols Note: Other options are not precluded Combination of multiple options are not precluded FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to aNB.
OPPO |  | Comment #1: Given the note saying “A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose”, the sensing RS in this proposal is actually based on “communication RS pattern”, instead of “RS for communication purpose”. Therefore, the main bullet should be make clearer as following: The following options can be considered to generate sensing RS based on communication RS pattern Comment #2: For Option 3, if any of comb factor {2,3,6} is not introduced in 6GR for communication purpose in communication agenda, this option seems to talk about something that is neither “based on RS for communication purpose” nor “based on RS with communication RS pattern”. It may be more appropriate to get this Option 3 into Proposal 4.2-1. Comment #3: It is not clear to us whether the “sensing RS” that is focused in this proposal is interpreted per Tx side or Rx side. For example, an aNB transmits one CSI-RS signal X1 for communication purpose only and another CSI-RS signal X2 for both communication+sensing purpose. If this proposal is provided from Tx perspective, it talks about X2 only; but if it is provided from Rx perspective, is there anything to prevent the Rx from including RS X1 as well (whether Rx does so would be an Rx implementation issue)? We believe RAN1 should not prevent the SRX to utilize a communication-purposed RS for sensing purpose, if doing so does not harm the sensing performance.
IDCC | Comment | The proposal is generally aligned with communication-RS-based sensing RS generation. However, the current wording under Option 1 and Option 2 may be interpreted as limiting the study to the listed RS types only. For this, we propose the following amendments: FL1][M] Proposal 4.1-1 The following options can be considered to generate sensing RS based on communication RS Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS The possible RS type includes but not limited to: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS Option 2: One or multiple communication RS of multiple RS types are combined as a sensing RS The possible RS type includes but not limited to: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS Option 3: Introduce comb factor {1,2,3,5,6,7} for CSI-RS in addition to {4,12,24}; other comb factor options are not precluded for further study Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols Other options are not precluded Combination of multiple options are not precluded FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].
vivo | Yes | One clarification question: when combining different communication RS(s) as sensing RS cases, non-uniformity will be taken into account for overall sensing RS overhead reduction—for example, by using different periodicities for different communication RS(s). Option 3&4 are not on the same page as Option 1&2. So maybe “option” can be replaced by “aspect”.
Xiaomi |  | Option 1 seems to be a sub-option of Option 2. But it seems ok to leave them separated. In addition to the listed options, we propose to also include a non-uniform sub-carrier pattern within a OFDM symbol in the study, to achieve fine range resolution and large unambiguous range with low overhead. This could for example be captured like below: Updated [FL1][M] Proposal 4.1-1 The following options can be considered to generate sensing RS based on communication RS Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS Option 2: One or multiple communication RS of multiple RS types are combined as a sensing RS The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS Option 3: Introduce comb factor {1,2,3,6} for CSI-RS in addition to {4, 12, 24} Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols Option 5: Non-uniform subcarrier pattern within an OFDM symbol Other options are not precluded Combination of multiple options are not precluded FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].

MediaTek | Comments | Regarding the Option 3, we share the similar view with OPPO that it’s so early to say that it is based on communication since there is not any decision on communication CSI-RS design. Our understanding is that if this RS is only used for sensing purpose, it belongs to the scope of sensing dedicated RS design. In addition, we think it’s premature to mention the QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS at current stage since there is on any details on the sensing RS design, and it is suggested to discuss it when there is clear sensing RS design. Therefore, we prefer to delete the bullet of “FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS”. Finally, the intention of the last Note bullet is not clear, whether some communication RS can be used for UE, it also needs to consider the UE capability, but it is in the communication discussion scope, or in other words, the communication procedure/configuration can already handle this issue, and there is no any specific discussion at sensing AI.
LGE |  | We propose this proposal should focus on sensing RS design based on communication RS pattern. When the study focuses on RS pattern rather than reuse of the RS signal itself, it remains open to consider DMRS- or PTRS-like patterns as candidates for sensing RS pattern design. Also it is not clear what is communication RS ‘type’. If RS type is implicitly categorized based on its relationship with data channel mapping, e.g., whether the RS is tightly coupled with data transmission, then the applicability of certain RS types for sensing becomes unclear. In particular, DMRS and PTRS are inherently associated with data channel transmission and mapping, and therefore their reuse as sensing RS signals should not be discussed independently. Instead, multiplexing between sensing RS and communication data should be clarified first, before considering reuse of such RS types for sensing purposes. Additionally, we suggest non-uniform RE pattern both in time/frequency domain, so please remove LGE from ‘Uniform in frequency and non-uniform in time’. We suggest modified proposal as follows: The following options can be considered to generate sensing RS pattern based on communication RS pattern Option 1: One or multiple communication RS patterns are combined as a sensing RS The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS Option 3: Introduce comb factor {1,2,3,6} for CSI-RS in addition to {4, 12, 24} Option 4: Non-uniform RE pattern in time/frequency domain for a sensing RS and uniform RE pattern in time/frequency domain Other options are not precluded Combination of multiple options are not precluded FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].

CMCC |  | We generally agree to the four options with PRS included in Option 1 and Option 2. Single-port transmission, where all TxRUs are mapped to a single port, is a common case for sensing to harness high antenna gain. PRS shall not be precluded at this stage.
Samsung | Comment | It doesn’t look like all 4 Options exclusive each other. There seem to have two different proposals – one about combining communication RSs for sensing purpose, and the other about time and frequency mappings. We are open to study Option 1 and 2, in general. We can consider different comb sizes other that the comb sizes for CSI-RS, bue do not think we should determine the exact comb size values at this point, which should be based on evaluation results. Regarding Option 4, we basically do not support changing communication RS RE patterns for sensing, which we think should be discussed in other agenda item. In general, regarding sensing RS RE mapping, the discussion should not be whether or not uniform or non-uniform mapping to avoid any further confusion, or the term ‘non-uniform’ should be further clarified. (Anyway if we combine two different types of RSs, then the resulting sensing RS mapping may look like non-uniform.) In other words, in our view, the point should not be about uniform or non-uniform OFDM or RE allocation. Instead, it should be about 1) minimum resolution of distance and velocity to support, 2) required minimum RS symbol distance (in time) and RE distance in frequency, 3) mechanism to reduce sensing RS overhead – we need to study how much we can reduce the RS overhead in time and frequency domain based on the evaluation.

[FL1][M] Proposal 4.1-1-rev1

The following options can be considered to generate the RE pattern of sensing RS based on communication RS from a sensing transmitter

Option 1: One or multiple communication RS of same RS type are combined as a sensing RS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

Option 2: One or multiple communication RS of multiple RS types are combined as a sensing RS

The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS

FFS: DL/UL DMRS, DL/UL PTRS

Option 3: Additional comb factor, e.g., 1,2,3,6 for CSI-RS in addition to existing comb factor 4, 12, 24.

Option 4: Non-uniform allocation of OFDM symbols for a sensing RS and uniform RE allocation in each of the OFDM symbols

Option 5: Non-uniform subcarrier pattern within an OFDM symbol

Other options are not precluded

Combination of multiple options are not precluded

FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS

Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Tejas Networks |  | We support Options 1, 2, 3 and 4 as a starting point for the study. Regarding QCL/TCI-State indication for sensing RS, we propose to deprioritize this aspect until the the sensing RS structure is clearer.
IDCC | Comment | We appreciate the clarification in rev1 and the relaxation of Option 3 wording. However, our main concern on RS type limitation is still not addressed. The current wording: “The possible RS type includes: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS” may still be interpreted as restricting the study scope to the explicitly listed RS types. To avoid unintended limitation of future study directions, we prefer the following modification for both Option 1 and Option 2 (note that QC also made this comment in previous round): “The possible RS type includes but is not limited to: CSI-RS, TRS, SRS” Otherwise, the proposal may prematurely constrain further study on other RS candidates and combinations.

Though the main issue for now is RE pattern in the proposal, QCL/TCI is always a key property for RS which impact how to detect/combine the multiple component RS for a sensing RS in the sensing processing. Since we cannot add all details in single proposal, I just add a FFS point here, which is definitely an issue for the full picture of sensing RS design. @ZTE, QC, IDCC: OK to try to include all RS types

NTT DOCOMO |  | For the purpose of enumerating possible solution directions, we can support Option 1 to 3 and 5. For option 4, we suggest changing “Non-uniform allocation” to “Non-uniform allocation or contiguous allocation” to cover the co-prime RS pattern. In addition, we propose to include the following option. Option 6: Time-staggered SRS symbol placement for sensing Enhancements may be considered for SRS-based sensing to support more flexible time-domain placement of SRS symbols. For example, the SRS starting symbol position may be varied across consecutive slots, and/or multiple SRS symbols within a slot may be placed non-contiguously. Such time-staggered SRS placement can increase the effective time-domain aperture of SRS observations for a given number of SRS REs. | For the purpose of enumerating possible solution directions, we can support Option 1 to 3 and 5. Option 6: Time-staggered SRS symbol placement for sensing Enhancements may be considered for SRS-based sensing to support more flexible time-domain placement of SRS symbols. For example, the SRS starting symbol position may be varied across consecutive slots, and/or multiple SRS symbols within a slot may be placed non-contiguously. Such time-staggered SRS placement can increase the effective time-domain aperture of SRS observations for a given number of SRS REs.

Option 5: Non-uniform subcarrier pattern within an OFDM symbol

Option 6: Time-staggered subcarrier mapping across symbols of communication RS

Other options are not precluded

FFS how to signal or indicate QCL/TCI-State of the sensing RS

Note: A communication RS being a component of the sensing RS may or may not be allocated to any UE for communication purpose which is up to [aNB, SF].

Dedicated sensing RS

Summary on company views

Study DL/UL dedicated RS for sensing which is not generated based on communication RS: HW, Xiaomi, DCM, QC, vivo, Apple, AT&T, LGE, Sharp, Sony, Aumovio, Panasonic, Honor, Google, Lenovo, SPRD, IDC, HNU, ETRI

NO: E//

If reusing communication RS is not sufficient: QC

First evaluate existing communication RS cannot fulfil KPI: vivo, Sony, Honor

Minimize impact to communication: DCM, LGE, NEC

QCL relationships with existing communication RS: Google

Solutions

Sequence good in auto- and cross-correlation, PAPR properties, robust detection, and good estimation accuracy: QC (FMCW chirp sequences)

pulsed or sensing-specific RS designs enable half-duplex operation: QC

sparse/non-uniform dedicated sensing RS: CMCC

a parameterized framework supporting configurable staggering, spacing, ports, and repetitions as a unified design for DL and UL: Apple

[Moderator’s note] Many companies are open to study dedicated sensing RS from scratch. However, it is not clear what will be the main candidate solutions. Above all, it seems commonly agreed that dedicated sensing RS design is only necessary is reusing communication RS with enhancement cannot meet KPIs.

Due to the limited remaining time for 6G study, RAN1 has to study dedicated RS in parallel with potential enhancement on communication RS reuse. The moderator hence suggest that interested companies can provide the detailed solution for dedicated sensing RS in next meeting, together with evaluation results. The focus should be the RE pattern, not the signalling details.

[Closed][FL1][M] Proposal 4.2-1

Companies are encouraged to bring up designs on dedicated sensing RS with performance evaluation/analysis in RAN1 #126.

Note: A dedicated sensing RS is only considered if a sensing RS that is generated based on communication RS with possible enhancements fails to meet performance objectives of sensing.

We suggest changed proposal as follows: Companies are encouraged to bring up dedicated sensing RS pattern with performance evaluation/analysis in RAN1 #126. Study the performance gap between a dedicated sensing RS and a sensing RS that is generated based on communication RS with possible enhancements. In addition, the optimal RE pattern design for dedicated sensing RS may depend on whether unused sensing resources can be utilized for communication. Therefore, the following options need to be considered for dedicated sensing RS pattern design: Alt 1: Resources unused for sensing RS can be utilized for communication. Alt 2: Resources unused for sensing RS cannot be utilized for communication. ​‌

For example, companies are discussing the waveform or sequence design for sensing in 10.8.3 and the motivation came from optimizing the sensing performance but the design might not be needed for comm and even degrading the comm performance if the same sequence/waveform is used for comm as well, so it is a trade-off. No need to struggle so much whether it is based on comm RS in proposal 4.1-1 or the dedicated RS (though we don’t know yet what it is). We just focus on the expected RS pattern in this agenda for sensing to meet the requirement.

Motivations to do LLS/SLS

[Moderator’s note] Though it is a bit overlap with 10.8.3, let’s collect motivation to do LLS/simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS.

[TABLE]
Agreement: (10.8.3) Companies are encouraged to provide views on necessity/pros/cons/metrics of LLS and/or SLS and pros/cons/metrics of theoretical waveform characteristics analysis, for evaluation of ISAC waveform and/or integration with communication, to RAN1#125 meeting.

Based on tdocs and discussions in Malta, there are 3 ways for the evaluation.

Option 1: LLS simulation based on new CDL-based LLS channel model for ISAC

Option 2: Simplified SLS

Potential simplification can be: single Tx and single Rx, LOS condition only, non-cooperative sensing, etc.

Option 3: Full SLS of cooperative sensing

Though Option 3 is always one possible candidate for evaluation in 10.8.2, concerns are expressed by multiple companies regarding its simulation time, not sensitive to a particular feature and hard to align/compare results from different companies. Therefore, Option 1 or 2 may be motivated.

In this discussion, let’s focus on the motivation for LLS/simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS. Please express your preference on the following two questions.

[FL1][H] Question 4.3-1

Do you prefer to run LLS or simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS?

No matter the answer is yes or no, please provide the reasons.

If “yes”, please indicate your preference on Option 1 or 2.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes/No | Reasons | Opt.1 or 2, if “Yes”

In our view, this approach facilitates reaching a consensus more efficiently within the limited time. Furthermore, the supporting simulation results for this option are more compelling. | Opt.2
OPPO | No | We suppose the RE pattern relates to the delay/range measurement, which is part of standard SLS. |

Full SLS (Option 3) may still be optionally used/reported for selected schemes/features if further verification is needed. | Option 2

Evaluations

Summary on company views

For study

Performance evaluations: HW, Apple, Panasonic, CATT

Overhead reduction: SS, Xiaomi, HW, MTK, Apple, CATT, OPPO

Communication impact: IDC, CATT

Sensing coverage

communication-compatible repetition structure: SS, E//

Metrics

Range estimation accuracy: CMCC, Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous Range: CMCC, Apple

Doppler estimation accuracy: CMCC, Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous Doppler: CMCC, Apple

Angle estimation accuracy: Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous angle: Apple

subject to probability of missed detection and probability of false alarm limit: Apple, Xiaomi

sidelobe peaks: Apple

[Moderator’s note] Since a simplified evaluation has only one sensing Tx and one sensing Rx, i.e., collaboration gain is not available, it is expected that the performance results may not meet all KPIs referring to evaluation on non-cooperative sensing in 5GA ISAC. When NLOS paths of multiple targets are modelled in the target channel, the FAP/MDP is likely to be problematic. One solution is to fix a FAP/MDP to a X percentage (X>5), and compare the difference of achieved sensing accuracy of different RE patterns.

Unless it is concluded that full SLS with cooperative sensing must be run to evaluate RE patterns of sensing RS, a discussion on a simulation methodology and related metrics will happen. Both sensing performance and resource overhead should be considered. To accelerate the progress, please provide your views on the following proposal.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.4-1

Companies are encouraged to bring up the RE patterns for sensing RS including the baseline RE pattern in RAN1 #126

The baseline RE pattern is a uniform RE pattern which maps to the grid of every M subcarriers and every N OFDM symbols. FFS M, N

Company should report whether/how the proposed RE patterns can be generated by reusing communication RS.

Study the achieved sensing performance of the RE patterns under the constraint of same upper bound of unambiguous range of delay/Doppler/angle

Compare at least the resource overhead, and the delay/Doppler/angle accuracy of the RE patterns when MDP is X percent and FAP is Y percent. FFS X, Y

No matter from a RS overhead perspective or a Doppler ambiguity perspective, such a 2D periodic RE pattern is a bad one. We can call it a benchmark RE pattern, but not baseline. [Moderator’s note] All contributions discuss measurement/report referring to the measurement levels agreed in 5GA ISAC. Even for UE measurement, some companies prefer to use measurement levels from 5GA ISAC as starting point too. The moderator suggest we refer to Level A/B/C/D in the measurement quantity related discussions, with the understanding that changes/updates to 5GA ISAC definition is possible for 6G ISAC.

Level A/B may be best for performance if fusion is done at the centre node, however, the key concern for level A/B is the extreme large payload size. Comparing level A and B, level A cannot outperform level B in any aspect. Referring on RAN3 progress in 5GA ISAC to remove level A, it is proposed to not consider level A for TRP/UE measurement in 6G ISAC.

Since this proposal is rather straightforward, please only comment if you have a concern on the proposal.

[FL3][H] Proposal 5.1-1

On TRP-side measurement, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not considered for 6G ISAC.

On UE-side measurement, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not considered for 6G ISAC.

Consequently, Level A should be disregarded for both TRP and UE-side measurements in the 6G ISAC framework.

[FL3][H] Proposal 5.1-1-rev1

On TRP-side measurement report, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not considered for 6G ISAC.

On UE-side measurement report, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not prioritized for 6G ISAC.

Measurement of a profile (Level B)

Summary on company views

Measurement of Level B: HW (TRP), CMCC(B2), OPPO, DCM, Apple (high capacity backhaul links with adequate synchronization), E// (B3), AT&T, NTHU (B1/B3), Jio, MTK, SPRD

Delay

absolute time of arrival for each sample in time: E//

Doppler

Angle

[Moderator’s note] 4 options of Level B are defined in 5GA ISAC. So down selection of the options is necessary.

A main drawback of level B is the large payload size. Therefore, payload size reduction must be considered as a key principle. For a dimension reported using a profile of delay, Doppler or angle domain, certain processing for overhead reduction is possible by limiting the report to a window on consecutive samples. As comparison, if it is reported in frequency domain, time domain (OFDM symbols) or antenna domain (MxN antenna ports), there is no way for compression. Base on the analysis, B3 would be mostly preferred.

On the other hand, CMCC gives an argument why reporting a Doppler profile is not preferred. I.e., UE has to buffer all sensing RS OFDM symbols in a CPI for Doppler processing, which requires a large buffer at UE and also cause a latency equal to CPI length. From this angle, B2 is preferrable.

In summary, as a first try for down selection, the moderator proposes to only consider B2 and B3 in future discussion.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.2-1

On measurement Level B, the SRX can report the complex values of one or multiple sets of consecutive samples. The following options are considered for further study

Option B2: Each set includes 2D consecutive samples in delay-angle profile per Tx antenna port per OFDM symbol

Option B3: Each set includes 3D consecutive samples in delay-Doppler-angle profile per Tx antenna port

FFS how to determine the number of samples in each set

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
ZTE |  | It is OK to study. Again, the overhead is too large especially for the cases of UE reporting via air interface. In addition, how to define Tx antenna port is an issue for sensing.
IDCC | No | The proposal to focus only on B2 and B3 may be too restrictive. It may be beneficial to also keep B1 and/or B4 for further study. Although B2/B3 can reduce overhead by reporting a selected window in the delay-angle or delay-Doppler-angle domain, their payload size can still become very large when the number of angle samples is high. For example, when Nangle is configured with a large value such as 360, the payload of B2/B3 may significantly or even extremely exceed the payload of B1/B4, depending on the assumed delay/Doppler window sizes and antenna-port configuration. Therefore, payload size should be evaluated across different parameter settings before down-selecting only B2 and B3. From this perspective, B1 and/or B4 should remain under consideration as potentially lower-overhead alternatives in some deployment scenarios or reporting configurations.
vivo | Yes | 
SONY | Yes | The FFS part can be generalized, such as FFS on how to reduce the overhead (e.g., determine the number of samples, window size, etc).
Xiaomi | [yes] | In principle this is fine, but also B2 and B3 result is large overhead. Overhead reduction techniques should be considered, e.g., differential reporting, or only report of samples exceeding a threshold Updated proposal: On measurement Level B, the SRX can report the complex values of one or multiple sets of consecutive samples. The following options are considered for further study Option B2: Each set includes 2D consecutive samples in delay-angle profile per Tx antenna port per OFDM symbol Option B3: Each set includes 3D consecutive samples in delay-Doppler-angle profile per Tx antenna port Overhead reduction techniques can be studied FFS how to determine the number of samples in each set
Qualcomm | No | We think it is premature to downselect a specific B option.
Huawei, HiSilicon | comment | Is this SRX is UE or TRP or both? Why are multiple sets needed to be reported?
Nokia |  | Can we simply say to re-use 5G B2/B3 defintions?
LGE |  | We support further study of Level B reporting based on Option B3. Assuming TSA information is provided to the sensing entity, reporting windows can be configured in association with the expected delay and angle ranges of the target. For Doppler, the reporting window should be able to cover the relevant Doppler range, including positive and negative Doppler values depending on the target mobility direction with respect to the sensing receiver. In addition, it is preferred to replace “set” with “window” for clearer interpretation. The following options are considered for further study Option B2: Each window includes 2D consecutive samples in delay-angle profile per Tx antenna port per OFDM symbol Option B3: Each window includes 3D consecutive samples in delay-Doppler-angle profile per Tx antenna port FFS how to determine the number of samples in each window

Measurement of a path (Level C)

Summary on company views

Measurement quantities

Delay: HW, E//, Lenovo, ZTE, LGE, Honor, NIST, Tiami, Ofinno, CEWiT, IIT

Absolute delay of Tx-target-Rx: vivo, OPPO

Differential delay between target path and reference path: OPPO, CATT, Xiaomi, vivo

Differential delay of Tx-target links from two Tx: vivo

Multi-RTT to measure the NLOS reference path: OPPO, vivo, Lenovo(round trip)

RSTD, RTOA: CATT

Doppler: HW, E//, OPPO, Lenovo, ZTE, LGE, Honor, NIST, Tiami, Ofinno, CEWiT, IIT

Angle: HW, E//, vivo, OPPO, ZTE, Honor, NIST, PCL, CEWiT

AOD: vivo, OPPO, HW, PCL

AOA: HW, E//, LGE, PCL

Multi-port RS for communication for AOD, e.g., CSI-RS: vivo, HW

AOD or AOA difference between target path and reference path: vivo

Amplitude, phase: QC

Power: HW

RSRPP: ZTE

Per path SINR: vivo (as confidence metric), OPPO

Per path RSRP/SINR: AT&T, OPPO

Confidence metric: CATT, ZTE, Jio

Micro-Doppler: vivo, IDCC, CEWiT

Anglular ambiguity: OPPO

Channel impulse response

Study reference for delay/Doppler/angle domain: E//

use 5G-A measurement definitions in TS 38.215 as a starting point: QC

Sensing SINR: Tiami, AT&T

RCS: [Tiami], IDC, Ofinno

Sensing SINR: Tiami

Moving target & Static object: HW

Cross-node synchronization: SS, CMCC, ETRI, Tiami, NIST, Lenovo, CU, IIT

[Moderator’s note] 5 Options are included in Level C in 5GA ISAC which is too much. Please check following comments from moderator to consolidate a solution.

Among the options, C1/C2/C3 is expressed as measurement per path, while C4/C5 is per point. Both work for TRP monostatic sensing. However, considering bistatic sensing, the following observation is made

If a UE is not capable for AOA estimation due to single or a smaller number of Rx antennas, the UE cannot determine the location of a scattering point even when delay/Doppler metric of a path are measured. So, it is not straightforward to name delay/Doppler measurements as metrics per point. However, it is always true to name it as delay/Doppler of a path.

In case sensing Tx/Rx is moving, the estimated Doppler at sensing Rx include the effects of movement of the sensing Tx/Rx besides that of the sensing target. Therefore, it is not strictly true to name the Doppler as a property of the scattering point or target. Again, there is no problem to name it as Doppler of the path.

In summary, considering bistatic sensing, the delay, Doppler and angle measurement if applicable are better to be defined per target path, i.e., C1/C2/C3. Such definition should universally applies to all sensing modes in 6G ISAC for consistency.

C1/C3/C4 uses metrics delay/range, Doppler/radial velocity and angle, while C2/C5 uses location and radial velocity. Still using the example that a UE is not capable for AOA estimation, the UE cannot determine the location of the scattering point in bistatic sensing mode, it is impossible to report location in C2/C5. In summary, C2/C5 cannot be applicable to all cases. C1/C3/C4 is desired for unified design.

Lastly, as to the choice between delay and range, or between Doppler and radial velocity, both work since they are exchangeable. However, delay/Doppler should be more preferable since they are the direct outcomes of a sensing algorithm.

In summary, the following proposal for Level C is made, i.e., the metrics for 6G ISAC should be defined as delay, Doppler, angle. Please provide your views.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.3-1

At least the following measurement metrics of a target path are considered for further study of Level C

Delay

Time difference between target path and reference path

RTT-like method for the target path

Doppler

Angle

AoA at receiver

AoD at transmitter

RSRP/SINR of target path

Quality/uncertainty/confidence

FFS ambiguity handling of delay/Doppler/angle

FFS how to define a path based on a subset or all delay/Doppler/angle parameters

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | The confidence metrics associated with sensing measurements—such as delay, Doppler, angle, and position—is crucial for ensuring robust data fusion in cooperative sensing frameworks.
ZTE |  | Is this only for UE reporting or for both TRP and UE reporting ? If this includes TRP reporting, why do we preclude some C2/C5? Even for UE reporting, we have UE devices such as CPE of which location may be fixed or known to aNB or SF. C2/C5 can still work well.
OPPO | A minor No | Comment #1: For the AoD at transmitter, the SRX needs to know the antenna panel information (such as inter-element spacing and panel orientation) which are normally not provided to SRX, especially if STX is aNB and SRX is UE. We provide a measurement type in our contribution that does not require the above information of STX antenna panel to be made known at SRX. So we propose to modify the “Angle” part as following: Angle AoA at receiver AoD at transmitter or measurements that can be used to derive “AoD at transmitter” and meanwhile does not require knowledge of transmitter antenna element spacing and panel orientation.
IDCC | Comment | The current metric set may not sufficiently capture sensing-specific characteristics needed for advanced sensing applications and richer target/path characterization. Consider including further study of additional sensing-specific measurement metrics, such as micro-Doppler-related characteristics and RCS/reflection-related metrics, while clarifying reference path definition/selection criteria. We propose the following amendments: [FL1][M] Proposal 5.3-1 At least the following measurement metrics of a target path are considered for further study of Level C Delay Time difference between target path and reference path (clarify reference path definition and selection criteria). RTT-like method for the target path Doppler/micro-Doppler Angle AoA at receiver AoD at transmitter RSRP/SINR/RCS of target path Quality/uncertainty/confidence FFS ambiguity handling of delay/Doppler/angle FFS how to define a path based on a subset or all delay/Doppler/angle parameters
vivo | Yes | For bistatic sensing, the definitions of delay/range and angle differ from those for monostatic sensing, and measurements related to UE privacy should be considered. The total delay is the sum of Tx–target and target–Rx delays. Separating these two components can enable better localization and privacy protection. The angle differences relative to the LOS path in bistatic plane are more natural for bistatic geometry than absolute AoA/AoD, as they reflect the bistatic geometry and can be measured even when the absolute orientation is unknown. We propose to add the following measurement metrics for further study: Delay/range delay/range of Tx-target delay/range of target-Rx Angle AoD difference in bistatic plane between LOS path and target path at transmitter AoA difference in bistatic plane between LOS path and target path at receiver
SONY | Yes | The above includes the necessary items (delay, angle, power, doppler and the quality).
Xiaomi | Yes | 
Qualcomm | No | For both UE and TRP, all options of level C should be studied. The options that are “per point” reporting, are definitely applicable for UEs. A UE can have multiple Rx antennas, and could do AoA estimation (e.g. it was already specified for SL Positionin gin Rel-18). A UE can be a CPE, in which case it can know its location very accurately.
CEWiT | Yes | The proposal includes all the metrics of a path.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Why is it ‘target path’? Is it saying the path targeted to be reported? Is this proposal saying TRP or UE or applied to both? Is it for TRP-UE bi-static or UE-TRP bi-static, right? Other comments: RTT-like is unclear, ‘Rx-Tx’ time difference is better. AoA/ZoA at receiver, AoD/ZoD at the transmitter is more accurate. Phase of the reported path can also be considered.
Nokia |  | Should we re-use our 5G Level C definitions?
LGE | No | We prefer to define Level C measurement based on a scattering point rather than a path. If a path is mainly distinguished by delay as in positioning measurements, ambiguity may remain in Doppler and angle domains. Therefore, point-based measurement is more suitable for representing delay/Doppler/angle-associated sensing results. In addition, NR measurement definitions have been mainly defined based on monostatic and further consideration is needed for bistatic sensing. In particular, the reference point may be different depending on the sensing mode, and therefore its definition should be clarified for monostatic and bistatic sensing. For angle measurement, the expressions “AoA at receiver” and “AoD at transmitter” may be not clear. It is preferred to clarify whether the angle is measured by the receiver or transmitter. For power-related metrics such as sensing SINR, it is also necessary to study how the interference level is defined and measured, since both target and non-target components may affect the SINR measurement. Therefore We suggest modifying the proposal as follows: [FL1][M] Proposal 5.3-1 At least the following measurement metrics of a target point are considered for further study of Level C Delay Time difference between target path and reference point FFS: how to define reference path for monostatic and bistatic sensing RTT-like method for the target point Doppler Angle AoA measured by receiver AoD measured by transmitter RSRP/SINR of target point FFS: How to define interference level Quality/uncertainty/confidence FFS ambiguity handling of delay/Doppler/angle FFS how to define a point based on a subset of interested delay/Doppler/angle parameters
Samsung |  | We are fine to consider path-level measurement as unified design for both monostatic sensing and bistatic sensing. Regarding delay, clarification on ‘reference path’ is needed. Regrading angle, we need to consider the case when Tx/Rx has only single antenna port.
PCL | Yes | Delay, Doppler, and Angle are the essential metrics. For Angle, we specifically support both AoA at the receiver and AoD at the transmitter. This is particularly important for sensing-assisted beam management, as these angular measurements can be directly mapped to beam directions.

At least the following measurement metrics of a target path are considered for further study of Level C

Time difference between a target path and reference path

Multi-RTT -like measurement for a target path

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
IDCC | Comment | We do not agree with moderator comment that RCS is not valid for level C and only level D, and are concerned by his comment on micro-Doppler being valid but not explicitly included – the work should be contribution driven and moderator should collect and reflect views impartially. Regarding RCS and micro-Doppler, there is a similar relationship to the already included Level-C path metrics. For example, Doppler is already considered as a path-level measurement, while micro-Doppler can be viewed as a finer-granularity Doppler-related characteristic associated with the path. Similarly, RSRP/SINR are already considered as path-level metrics, while RCS can be viewed as a reflection-related characteristic derived from path measurements such as received path power, path loss, and propagation geometry. Therefore, from our perspective, RCS and micro-Doppler can also be measured or estimated for a detected path at Level C, even before final target/object interpretation at Level D. Such sensing-specific characteristics may provide important information for distinguishing target-related paths from surrounding environmental reflections or clutter, and hence benefit the selection or identification of target paths for subsequent Level-D measurements and processing. In addition, while we understand that “at least” does not preclude additional metrics, we believe explicitly mentioning micro-Doppler and RCS would provide clearer study direction for sensing-oriented path characterization in Level-C measurements. We propose the following modification: Doppler/micro-Doppler RSRP/SINR/RCS of target path

Measurement of a scattering point (Level D)

Summary on company views

Level D

Power

RCS: ZTE, Tiami, IDC, Ofinno

A list of RSRPP measured by multiple TRPs: ZTE

RSRPP in 2D RD bin: ZTE

RSRPP in 1D delay bin: ZTE

Confidence metric: CATT

Uncertainty shape + confidence probability: ZTE (TS 23.032)

[Moderator’s note] The definition of Level D is relative clear in 5GA ISAC. The sensing receiver is assumed to have capability to identify a target with single or multiple scattering points. Instead of using multiple options, a general framework could be to define Level D as a method to report measurement results per scattering point. Further, association of measurement across CPI is likely to be a capability especially for a UE.

As to power, I add “if applicable” as the agreement of 5GA ISAC.

Companies are encouraged to provide views on the following proposal.

Power if applicable

This is more a matter of architecture and required assistance data to CN from our perspective, since there is no new impact on measurement reporting or on RS configuration. From our perspective, the important outcome of level D evaluation is the required assistance information, which should be clearly identified before feasibility can be assessed by other RAN WG regarding signalling.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Is it for TRP or UE or both? Ok to study but we have strong concern to have UE location reported to aNB and aNB location reported to UE.

QC: RAN1 should study ISAC measurement reporting by abstracting periodic, semi-persistent, and aperiodic reporting as reporting behaviors defined by configurable parameters (e.g., periodicity, start/end conditions, and number of reporting instances), without pre-judging the protocol layer or activation mechanism used to realize such reporting.

reporting latency: SS

Quantization: CT, Tejas (adaptive compressions)

UE measurement report per Rx beam: E//

UE capability: SS, ETRI, Google

sensing processing timeline/unit (SPU): SPRD, Honor, NEC

CATT: A hybrid approach may also be considered, where time-critical metrics are reported via Alt 2, while large-payload, delay-tolerant data (e.g., environmental reconstruction) is reported via Alt 1

SS: An environmental sensing phase can be configured for a UE to analyze channel and environmental factors (e.g., multipath, blockage, fading, clutter, and interference), improving target detection and identification, with measurements optionally reported to the aNB or SF and processing details left to UE implementation.

[Moderator’s note] Option 1A and 1B are listed for study in the agreement of RAN1 #124bis. Multiple companies provide discussions on the two options. Based on the inputs, the following observation is made

Option 1A

Pros

Low latency of sensing measurement, report and computation

High accuracy by avoiding the loss of information during quantization on sensing data from aNB

Efficient scheduling/control of mutual assistance of sensing and communication

Cons

Privacy concern if certain sensitive information of UE is reported to aNB

Option 1B

Pros

NR LPP can be a reference for the design.

Reporting assistance information of UE to sensing function is often considered as no/less privacy issue

Cons

Larger latency since necessary measurement from UE needs to go to SF first, then go to aNB after SF processing.

Instead of rushing down selection, it is proposed that RAN1 can study RAN1 impacts of both options, since the final choice requires cross-WG coordination and maybe only possible at the end of or after the study item.

For Option 1A (UE-aNB), based on the inputs, it is proposed to study the configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource, and/or measurement report, as well as payload size reduction of the report. The same proposal is made for Option 1B (UE-SF), but the discussion should be limited to issues/solutions from RAN1 perspective.

[FL1][H] Proposal 5.5-1

For UE as sensing receiver, assuming UE measurements is directly sent to aNB (Option 1A), the following aspects can be considered for further study

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement report

Payload size reduction of measurement report

Other aspects are not precluded

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement report

Payload size reduction of measurement report

Other aspects are not precluded

Xiaomi |  | Yes to 5.5-1 and 5.5.-2
Qualcomm | No | The reporting endpoint for Option 1A (UE-to-aNB) and Option 1B (UE-to-SF) is for other working groups to decide and does not necessarily need to be concluded in RAN1 at this stage. By splitting it up, it appears as if we have agreed to have separate solutions, or that we understand what “sent directly to anB” means. For some companies “directly” means RRC, but for others it may just mean that there is a higher layer transport protocol with an end-point to the aNB. All these are architecture debates and should not happen in RAN1; they are already happening in RAN2/SA2. Therefore, we support IDCC’s proposal: For UE as sensing receiver, regardless of the reporting endpoint (e.g., assuming UE measurements is sent to aNB or SF) (Option 1B), the following aspects can be considered for further study from RAN1 perspective There is no need to separate the discussion, since the transport of the measurements and/or assistance information should be left up to the other WGs. With regards to the 1A and 1B, both proposals can be merged for now to a potential agreement that says: From RAN1 perspective, UE measurements and/or assistance information can be sent to aNB and/or SF; RAN2 and SA2 to study and decide the transport And we don’t further debate what “directly” means and what it doesn’t mean, without waiting for progress from RAN2/RAN3/SA2.
CEWiT | Yes | For option 1A and option 1B discuss the sensing use cases. Regarding measurement reporting from UE segregate the L1,L2 and L3 reporting based on key metrics
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | The proposals are really unclear what should be studied. Are we studying how to configure, activate/deactivate or dynamically indicate the resource or the measurement? Or are we studying the necessity to do those? I’d tend to believe the intension is to study the necessity because the ‘how’ issue should be details to be fixed in WI. Then the question is whether they are the issues to study at the beginning of study phase. For example, if we need to study whether to do activation/deactivation and dynamic indication is needed, we should study/discuss the motivation first. At this moment, we don’t know yet or not ready yet to study that aspect because the overall resources needed for sensing is unclear yet. It is unclear yet what the resource will be used for, for detection only, for assisting comm only, or for both? We don’t know yet the reported should be dynamically reported or other ways because we don’t know yet what cases we will discuss it for, for assisting comm, for BM or for CSI, may or may not be the same required reporting behavior. Overall, we still believe such proposals are not helpful for guiding the study.

If we consider using communication RS for sensing, it may be natural to reuse the configuration/activation designed for communication RS for sensing as well for better integration. On the other hand, Option 1B may be suitable for the use case that can tolerate large latency, e.g., environment construction. For Option 1B, even if the measurement report is carried via higher layers, the allocation of physical measurement resources can still be discussed within RAN1.
PCL | Yes | We support studying both options. However, for sensing-assisted communication (e.g., beam management), we see a clear advantage for Option 1A (UE to aNB). The lower latency of L1 reporting is critical for proactive beam tracking and fast beam failure recovery. Option 1B introduces significant latency (via SF), which may undermine the benefit of sensing assistance for dynamic procedures.

How the measurement is triggered / reported is not up to RAN1 unless we discuss L1 measurement reports. This not different for UE side or TRP side measurements. We can discuss configuration of the resources and their definitions, but measurement requests / reports beyond L1 is out of RAN1 scope.

[FL3][H] Proposal 5.5-1-rev1

For UE as sensing receiver, the proponents should provide motivations for measurement report and assistance information from UE to aNB (Option 1A) and from UE to SF (Option 1B) in RAN1 #126, at least from the following aspects from RAN1 perspective

Latency of sensing resource allocation and of sensing measurement/report

Impact to sensing performance for object detection/tracking

Impact to the communication performance assistance assisted by sensing

The amount of measurement data vs. the maximum payload of L1/L2/high layer report

Privacy of TRP and UE

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
IDCC | Comment | 1- From our perspective, several aspects listed in the proposal appear weakly related or not directly related to the topic of UE measurement/reporting mechanisms themselves. In particular: latency of sensing resource allocation, impact to sensing performance for object detection/tracking, impact to communication performance assisted by sensing, and privacy of TRP, are broader sensing-system or sensing-assisted-communication considerations, rather than issues specific to UE measurement/reporting mechanisms or reporting endpoint selection. For example, sensing performance for object detection/tracking mainly depends on sensing RS design, sensing processing, sensing geometry, sensing algorithms, and sensing deployment assumptions. Similarly, communication-assistance performance depends on the sensing-assisted communication framework and sensing accuracy assumptions, not only on the UE reporting mechanism itself. Therefore, it may be preferable to better scope the proposal toward aspects directly related to UE measurement/reporting behavior and reporting framework impacts from RAN1 perspective. We may also consider adding the following discussion point: Definition of sensing-specific events and event conditions (e.g., detection, tracking, mobility/state changes, threshold crossing, object appearance/disappearance) according to the sensing use case and measurement level (e.g., Level B/C/D), including potential payload-size reduction mechanisms for sensing measurement reports.

[Moderator’s note] It is controversial to report TRP location and/or UE location. So, it is proposed that RAN1 can continue discussions.

The expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity as assistance information were proposed in 5GA ISAC, and it is reported by multiple companies in current SI again. Since it has clear benefit to reduce the SRX processing complexity, the moderator proposes to agree on such assistance information.

Further, due to the limited inputs, no other commonly preferred parameter is found. Therefore, the moderator encourages all interested companies can provides views on necessary assistance information in next meeting.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.7-1

The information on the expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity of interested target/object can be sent from SF to aNB, from SF to UE, or from aNB to UE

Companies are encouraged to provide input of other assistance information exchanged between SF, aNB and UE in RAN1 #126.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | Assistance information provided by the network (SF/aNB) to Tx/Rx nodes is essential to constrain the sensing search space, thereby reducing latency, power consumption, and implementation complexity. In particular, for bistatic sensing, environmental information such as the LOS path between the Tx and Rx nodes can be utilized as a reference to mitigate time synchronization errors.

IDCC | Comment | We note that rev1 partially reflects our previous comments regarding UE/TRP location information as assistance information. We still believe that further study of sensing-specific assistance information may be beneficial to improve sensing processing efficiency and sensing accuracy. In particular, target-specific assistance information associated with interested sensing targets/objects may be useful, e.g., target characteristics, target mobility behavior, clutter-related information, or sensing-region-related constraints. In addition to UE/TRP location information, we agree with ZTE and vivo that STX antenna/panel configuration information may also be important assistance information. We also align with Samsung that assistance information aimed at reducing UE processing complexity and reporting overhead may be considered. Furthermore, we share the view of Qualcomm and OPPO that the discussion does not necessarily need to focus on specific entities (e.g., SF, aNB, UE), but rather on the procedural aspects and the types of assistance information exchanged. We propose the following changes. Companies are encouraged to provide input of other assistance information exchanged between SF, aNB and UE in RAN1 #126. | We note that rev1 partially reflects our previous comments regarding UE/TRP location information as assistance information. We still believe that further study of sensing-specific assistance information may be beneficial to improve sensing processing efficiency and sensing accuracy. In particular, target-specific assistance information associated with interested sensing targets/objects may be useful, e.g., target characteristics, target mobility behavior, clutter-related information, or sensing-region-related constraints. In addition to UE/TRP location information, we agree with ZTE and vivo that STX antenna/panel configuration information may also be important assistance information. We also align with Samsung that assistance information aimed at reducing UE processing complexity and reporting overhead may be considered. Furthermore, we share the view of Qualcomm and OPPO that the discussion does not necessarily need to focus on specific entities (e.g., SF, aNB, UE), but rather on the procedural aspects and the types of assistance information exchanged. We propose the following changes.
