Dedicated sensing RS

Summary on company views

Study DL/UL dedicated RS for sensing which is not generated based on communication RS: HW, Xiaomi, DCM, QC, vivo, Apple, AT&T, LGE, Sharp, Sony, Aumovio, Panasonic, Honor, Google, Lenovo, SPRD, IDC, HNU, ETRI

NO: E//

If reusing communication RS is not sufficient: QC

First evaluate existing communication RS cannot fulfil KPI: vivo, Sony, Honor

Minimize impact to communication: DCM, LGE, NEC

QCL relationships with existing communication RS: Google

Solutions

Sequence good in auto- and cross-correlation, PAPR properties, robust detection, and good estimation accuracy: QC (FMCW chirp sequences)

pulsed or sensing-specific RS designs enable half-duplex operation: QC

sparse/non-uniform dedicated sensing RS: CMCC

a parameterized framework supporting configurable staggering, spacing, ports, and repetitions as a unified design for DL and UL: Apple

[Moderator’s note] Many companies are open to study dedicated sensing RS from scratch. However, it is not clear what will be the main candidate solutions. Above all, it seems commonly agreed that dedicated sensing RS design is only necessary is reusing communication RS with enhancement cannot meet KPIs.

Due to the limited remaining time for 6G study, RAN1 has to study dedicated RS in parallel with potential enhancement on communication RS reuse. The moderator hence suggest that interested companies can provide the detailed solution for dedicated sensing RS in next meeting, together with evaluation results. The focus should be the RE pattern, not the signalling details.

[Closed][FL1][M] Proposal 4.2-1

Companies are encouraged to bring up designs on dedicated sensing RS with performance evaluation/analysis in RAN1 #126.

Note: A dedicated sensing RS is only considered if a sensing RS that is generated based on communication RS with possible enhancements fails to meet performance objectives of sensing.

Motivations to do LLS/SLS

[Moderator’s note] Though it is a bit overlap with 10.8.3, let’s collect motivation to do LLS/simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS.

[TABLE]
Agreement: (10.8.3) Companies are encouraged to provide views on necessity/pros/cons/metrics of LLS and/or SLS and pros/cons/metrics of theoretical waveform characteristics analysis, for evaluation of ISAC waveform and/or integration with communication, to RAN1#125 meeting.

Based on tdocs and discussions in Malta, there are 3 ways for the evaluation.

Option 1: LLS simulation based on new CDL-based LLS channel model for ISAC

Option 2: Simplified SLS

Potential simplification can be: single Tx and single Rx, LOS condition only, non-cooperative sensing, etc.

Option 3: Full SLS of cooperative sensing

Though Option 3 is always one possible candidate for evaluation in 10.8.2, concerns are expressed by multiple companies regarding its simulation time, not sensitive to a particular feature and hard to align/compare results from different companies. Therefore, Option 1 or 2 may be motivated.

In this discussion, let’s focus on the motivation for LLS/simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS. Please express your preference on the following two questions.

[FL1][H] Question 4.3-1

Do you prefer to run LLS or simplified SLS for the evaluation of RE patterns of sensing RS?

No matter the answer is yes or no, please provide the reasons.

If “yes”, please indicate your preference on Option 1 or 2.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes/No | Reasons | Opt.1 or 2, if “Yes”
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | While we are generally open to both Options 1 and 2, if a down-selection is required, we prefer Simplified SLS. In our view, this approach facilitates reaching a consensus more efficiently within the limited time. Furthermore, the supporting simulation results for this option are more compelling. | Opt.2
OPPO | No | We suppose the RE pattern relates to the delay/range measurement, which is part of standard SLS. | 
IDCC | Yes | Simplified SLS (our preference) can preserve the essential sensing processing behaviour while significantly reducing simulation complexity and execution time compared to full SLS.Since the evaluation of RE patterns mainly targets relative performance comparison among candidate designs, simplified assumptions (e.g., single Tx/Rx, LOS-only conditions, non-cooperative sensing) are considered sufficient for baseline evaluation. Full SLS (Option 3) may still be optionally used/reported for selected schemes/features if further verification is needed. | Option 2
vivo | No | We don’t see strong motivation to run LLS at this moment. | 
SONY | Yes | In our view, at least the theoretical analysis can be provided. In case, we need to compare with the results from different companies, Option 2 is a good compromise. | Option 2
Xiaomi | Yes | For simulation simplicity. We can evaluate more cases in shorter and still assess the sensing performance. | Option 1
Qualcomm | No | SLS is preferred for evaluation of RE patterns and no need to have simplified SLS or LLS | 
CEWiT | Yes | It is easy to generate the simulation | Option 2

With that, we think SLS should be used. | Opt3.

For the sensing SLS, usually dropping targets is the initial step for simulation. If this step is still included in the “simplified SLS”, then we have a problem for link level simulation. | Either Option 1 or 2 is fine; however, Option 2 needed to be defined.
LGE | Yes | We think that LLS helps evaluating different RS patterns, regardless of LLS implementation method. We slightly prefer option 1, with ISAC LLS channel model based on the CDL-D model. We also open to option 2 | Opt. 1
Apple |  | Yes | Option 2
CMCC |  | Theoretical analysis is indispensable. Among the proposed schemes, we prefer Option 1, as it enables flexible SNR adjustment and involves fewer scenario-related parameters. | 
Samsung |  | We slightly prefer Option 1. RE pattern design directly impacts the sensing signal/waveform and the corresponding signal processing. Therefore, LLS is suitable to evaluate these signal-level characteristics, such as the impact of range/Doppler resolution and ambiguity behavior for different RE patterns. | 
Tejas Networks | Yes |  | Option 2
ETRI | Yes | LLS is preferred because the RE pattern of the sensing RS directly determines link-level metrics, unambiguous range/Doppler, resolution, ambiguity function sidelobes, processing gain, and CRLB, which can only be evaluated by running the actual sensing signal processing on the RS pattern itself. | Option 1

[Closed][FL1][H] Question 4.3-2

If LLS or simplified SLS is used to evaluate RE patterns of sensing RS, do you think it is still necessary to run full SLS of cooperative sensing for the same RE patterns?

[TABLE]
Company | Yes/No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | No | If LLS or simplified SLS is adopted to evaluate sensing RS RE patterns, the necessity for full SLS is obviated. Alternatively, we could perform full SLS at the initial stage to establish a baseline, thereby eliminating the need for further discussion on LLS or simplified SLS results.
IDCC | No | Simplified SLS is considered sufficient as the baseline evaluation methodology for RE pattern assessment. Full SLS may still be used/reported for selected schemes/features if further verification is needed, for example cooperative sensing schemes involving multiple sensing nodes/TRPs.
SONY |  | It can be as an optional option for the evaluation.
Xiaomi | No | It is sufficient to use with SLS for baseline performance including cooperative sensing and for a baseline RE pattern. The baseline pattern and modified pattern can be compared with each other with LLS/simplified SLS
CEWiT | No | It may not be necessary
Nokia | No | 
LGE | No | If cooperative sensing corresponds to fusion of multiple sensing results, full SLS is not required, as LLS or simplified SLS is sufficient for RE pattern evaluation. However, for scenarios involving neighboring-cell sensing entities (e.g., inter-cell or multi-TRP sensing), full SLS may be needed to capture network-level effects. However full SLS would require additional interference modeling, deployment scenario, sensing entity association and etc. Therefore, it is preferred to deprioritize full SLS at this stage.
Apple | No | 
CMCC | No | Given the limited time, full SLS of cooperative sensing is unnecessary. Moreover, cooperative sensing algorithms exert a far greater impact on overall performance than specific signal patterns.
Samsung | No | 
Tejas Networks | No | It is not necessary to run full SLS of cooperative sensing to evaluate RE patterns.
Cohere | Yes | SLS is required for evaluation (e.g. of interference and clutter removal).
Moderator |  | It seems the views is quite aligned. The issue is closed

Evaluations

Summary on company views

For study

Performance evaluations: HW, Apple, Panasonic, CATT

Overhead reduction: SS, Xiaomi, HW, MTK, Apple, CATT, OPPO

Communication impact: IDC, CATT

Sensing coverage

communication-compatible repetition structure: SS, E//

Metrics

Range estimation accuracy: CMCC, Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous Range: CMCC, Apple

Doppler estimation accuracy: CMCC, Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous Doppler: CMCC, Apple

Angle estimation accuracy: Apple, Xiaomi

Unambiguous angle: Apple

subject to probability of missed detection and probability of false alarm limit: Apple, Xiaomi

sidelobe peaks: Apple

[Moderator’s note] Since a simplified evaluation has only one sensing Tx and one sensing Rx, i.e., collaboration gain is not available, it is expected that the performance results may not meet all KPIs referring to evaluation on non-cooperative sensing in 5GA ISAC. When NLOS paths of multiple targets are modelled in the target channel, the FAP/MDP is likely to be problematic. One solution is to fix a FAP/MDP to a X percentage (X>5), and compare the difference of achieved sensing accuracy of different RE patterns.

Unless it is concluded that full SLS with cooperative sensing must be run to evaluate RE patterns of sensing RS, a discussion on a simulation methodology and related metrics will happen. Both sensing performance and resource overhead should be considered. To accelerate the progress, please provide your views on the following proposal.

[FL1][H] Proposal 4.4-1

Companies are encouraged to bring up the RE patterns for sensing RS including the baseline RE pattern in RAN1 #126

The baseline RE pattern is a uniform RE pattern which maps to the grid of every M subcarriers and every N OFDM symbols. FFS M, N

Company should report whether/how the proposed RE patterns can be generated by reusing communication RS.

Study the achieved sensing performance of the RE patterns under the constraint of same upper bound of unambiguous range of delay/Doppler/angle

Compare at least the resource overhead, and the delay/Doppler/angle accuracy of the RE patterns when MDP is X percent and FAP is Y percent. FFS X, Y

[TABLE]
Company | Yes/No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | We agree on defining a baseline RE pattern. Additionally, companies should explicitly report the specific methodology used to generate their proposed RE patterns.
OPPO | No | Comment #1: We do not agree a RE pattern of “every M subcarriers and every N symbols” is named a baseline pattern. No matter from a RS overhead perspective or a Doppler ambiguity perspective, such a 2D periodic RE pattern is a bad one. We can call it a benchmark RE pattern, but not baseline. Comment #2: The 2nd bullet reads confusing. The 2nd main bullet talks about comparing different performances (MDP/FAP/accuracy) based on the same upper-bound of unambiguous ranges on delay/Doppler/angle, which is somehow linked to the RE mapping pattern; but its sub-bullet talks about comparing the overhead and accuracy for the given MDP/FAP.
IDCC | Comment | We propose the following amendment: FL1][H] Proposal 4.4-1 Companies are encouraged to bring up the RE patterns for sensing RS including the baseline RE pattern in RAN1 #126 The baseline RE pattern is a uniform RE pattern which maps to the grid of every M subcarriers and every N OFDM symbols. FFS M, N Company should report whether/how the proposed RE patterns can be generated by reusing communication RS. Study the achieved sensing performance of the RE patterns under the constraint of same upper bound of unambiguous range of delay/Doppler/angle Compare at least the resource overhead, and the delay/Doppler/angle accuracy of the RE patterns when MDP is X percent and FAP is Y percent. FFS X, Y Note: Angle-related metrics may be excluded from baseline evaluation of sensing RS RE patterns, since angle performance mainly depends on antenna/array, beamforming, and spatial processing assumptions.

Xiaomi | Yes | A baseline pattern is important for performance evaluation, so that different companies who may have different RE patterns have a common baseline to compare their respective results.
Qualcomm | No | We can focus first on the communication RS reuse and then treat this proposal.

Meeting the sensing requirement should be the design target to avoid the overdesign in 6G DAY1, so the study should not be only looking at the relative gain between uniform or non-uniform.
Nokia | Yes | 
MediaTek | Comment | Regarding the wording of “reusing communication RS” at the 2nd sub-bullet of the first main bullet, it needs to clarify is it to reuse the existing communication RS type/pattern or reuse the combination of different RS type/pattern as discussed at Proposal 4.1-1? In addition, regarding the sensing metric at 1st sub-bullet of the second main bullet, we suggest it is needs to firstly discuss what’s definition of sensing resource overhead, sensing MDP and sensing FAP since there are no any agreement on these definition at 6G RAN1 study for now.

Samsung |  | As we provided our response to proposal 4.1-1, we should be careful about using the terminology ‘uniform’ RE pattern as companies may have their own understanding. The every M subcarriers and N symbols mapping should be as a benchmark, as OPPO indicated, but M and N depends on the use cases, target detection object range and speed. When determining M and N values, angle accuracy should not be considered. Additionally, we think it is also important to compare the processing complexity for using the signal associated with the proposed RE patterns for sensing.

Meeting the sensing requirement should be the design target to avoid the overdesign in 6G DAY1, so the study should not be only looking at the relative gain between uniform or non-uniform.

Measurement/report

Measurement levels

Summary on company views

TRP measurement:

Level A: CMCC, Apple (high capacity backhaul links with adequate synchronization)

NO: E//, SPRD, Sony, DCM

Level B: HW, CMCC(B2), OPPO, DCM, SPRD, NTHU (B1/B3), Apple (high capacity backhaul links with adequate synchronization)

CMCC: a group of digital beamformer related to complete orthogonal DFT basis for B2

Level C: HW, OPPO, LGE(baseline), Panasonic, Apple, DCM, QC(TRP assisted), NIST, SPRD, CT, Google

Level D: OPPO, LGE, Panasonic, Apple, DCM, QC(TRP based), NIST, CT, Google, ZTE(D3)

Study the levels for each sensing mode and use case: DCM

TRP side measurement: TRP-based vs. TRP-assisted: QC

Privacy concern: vivo

UE location or velocity are needed for gNB target detection:vivo

TRP location and antenna information are needed for SF calculate angels/position:vivo

CMCC: Level-C and level-D are not compatible with communication assistance use case.

UE measurement

NR ISAC measurement levels as baseline: CMCC

Level A: CMCC, MTK

NO: ETRI, E//, Xiaomi, DCM, SPRD

Level B: E//, CMCC, DCM, AT&T, NTHU (B1/B3), Jio, MTK, SPRD

B3: E// (detected paths and surroundings)

NO: Apple

Level C: CATT, OPPO, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE(baseline), Panasonic, Google, Tiami, ZTE, HW, Nokia, DCM, NEC, IDC, MTK, CU, Honor, NIST, AT&T, Jio

NO: E//, CMCC

E//: target or a point, or paths identification should be left to SF

CMCC: lack of unified signal processing criteria lead to degraded data fusion performance

Level D: CATT, OPPO, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE, Panasonic, Google, Tiami, Nokia, DCM, SPRD (D3, D4), NEC, IDC, CT, MTK, CU, NIST, Jio

NO: E//, CMCC

E//: target or a point, or paths identification should be left to SF

CMCC: Reliable Level-D measurement is difficult to obtain at the UE side

How to overcome privacy concern: vivo

UE side measurement: UE-based vs. UE-assisted: QC, AT&T

ZTE: 6G network are proposed to be able to request UE sensing measurement report as well as location and orientation for sensing and sensing assisting communication while protecting UE’s privacy

[Moderator’s note] All contributions discuss measurement/report referring to the measurement levels agreed in 5GA ISAC. Even for UE measurement, some companies prefer to use measurement levels from 5GA ISAC as starting point too. The moderator suggest we refer to Level A/B/C/D in the measurement quantity related discussions, with the understanding that changes/updates to 5GA ISAC definition is possible for 6G ISAC.

Level A/B may be best for performance if fusion is done at the centre node, however, the key concern for level A/B is the extreme large payload size. Comparing level A and B, level A cannot outperform level B in any aspect. Referring on RAN3 progress in 5GA ISAC to remove level A, it is proposed to not consider level A for TRP/UE measurement in 6G ISAC.

Since this proposal is rather straightforward, please only comment if you have a concern on the proposal.

[FL3][H] Proposal 5.1-1

On TRP-side measurement, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not considered for 6G ISAC.

On UE-side measurement, measurement Level A from NR ISAC is not considered for 6G ISAC.

Measurement of a profile (Level B)

Summary on company views

Measurement of Level B: HW (TRP), CMCC(B2), OPPO, DCM, Apple (high capacity backhaul links with adequate synchronization), E// (B3), AT&T, NTHU (B1/B3), Jio, MTK, SPRD

Delay

absolute time of arrival for each sample in time: E//

Doppler

Angle

[Moderator’s note] 4 options of Level B are defined in 5GA ISAC. So down selection of the options is necessary.

A main drawback of level B is the large payload size. Therefore, payload size reduction must be considered as a key principle. For a dimension reported using a profile of delay, Doppler or angle domain, certain processing for overhead reduction is possible by limiting the report to a window on consecutive samples. As comparison, if it is reported in frequency domain, time domain (OFDM symbols) or antenna domain (MxN antenna ports), there is no way for compression. Base on the analysis, B3 would be mostly preferred.

On the other hand, CMCC gives an argument why reporting a Doppler profile is not preferred. I.e., UE has to buffer all sensing RS OFDM symbols in a CPI for Doppler processing, which requires a large buffer at UE and also cause a latency equal to CPI length. From this angle, B2 is preferrable.

In summary, as a first try for down selection, the moderator proposes to only consider B2 and B3 in future discussion.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.2-1

On measurement Level B, the SRX can report the complex values of one or multiple sets of consecutive samples. The following options are considered for further study

Option B2: Each set includes 2D consecutive samples in delay-angle profile per Tx antenna port per OFDM symbol

Option B3: Each set includes 3D consecutive samples in delay-Doppler-angle profile per Tx antenna port

FFS how to determine the number of samples in each set

IDCC | No | The proposal to focus only on B2 and B3 may be too restrictive. It may be beneficial to also keep B1 and/or B4 for further study. Although B2/B3 can reduce overhead by reporting a selected window in the delay-angle or delay-Doppler-angle domain, their payload size can still become very large when the number of angle samples is high. For example, when Nangle is configured with a large value such as 360, the payload of B2/B3 may significantly or even extremely exceed the payload of B1/B4, depending on the assumed delay/Doppler window sizes and antenna-port configuration. Therefore, payload size should be evaluated across different parameter settings before down-selecting only B2 and B3. From this perspective, B1 and/or B4 should remain under consideration as potentially lower-overhead alternatives in some deployment scenarios or reporting configurations.

Measurement of a path (Level C)

Summary on company views

Doppler: HW, E//, OPPO, Lenovo, ZTE, LGE, Honor, NIST, Tiami, Ofinno, CEWiT, IIT

AOD: vivo, OPPO, HW, PCL

Sensing SINR: Tiami

Moving target & Static object: HW

Cross-node synchronization: SS, CMCC, ETRI, Tiami, NIST, Lenovo, CU, IIT

[Moderator’s note] 5 Options are included in Level C in 5GA ISAC which is too much. Please check following comments from moderator to consolidate a solution.

Among the options, C1/C2/C3 is expressed as measurement per path, while C4/C5 is per point. Both work for TRP monostatic sensing. However, considering bistatic sensing, the following observation is made

If a UE is not capable for AOA estimation due to single or a smaller number of Rx antennas, the UE cannot determine the location of a scattering point even when delay/Doppler metric of a path are measured. So, it is not straightforward to name delay/Doppler measurements as metrics per point. However, it is always true to name it as delay/Doppler of a path.

In case sensing Tx/Rx is moving, the estimated Doppler at sensing Rx include the effects of movement of the sensing Tx/Rx besides that of the sensing target. Therefore, it is not strictly true to name the Doppler as a property of the scattering point or target. Again, there is no problem to name it as Doppler of the path.

In summary, considering bistatic sensing, the delay, Doppler and angle measurement if applicable are better to be defined per target path, i.e., C1/C2/C3. Such definition should universally applies to all sensing modes in 6G ISAC for consistency.

C1/C3/C4 uses metrics delay/range, Doppler/radial velocity and angle, while C2/C5 uses location and radial velocity. Still using the example that a UE is not capable for AOA estimation, the UE cannot determine the location of the scattering point in bistatic sensing mode, it is impossible to report location in C2/C5. In summary, C2/C5 cannot be applicable to all cases. C1/C3/C4 is desired for unified design.

Lastly, as to the choice between delay and range, or between Doppler and radial velocity, both work since they are exchangeable. However, delay/Doppler should be more preferable since they are the direct outcomes of a sensing algorithm.

In summary, the following proposal for Level C is made, i.e., the metrics for 6G ISAC should be defined as delay, Doppler, angle. Please provide your views.

At least the following measurement metrics of a target path are considered for further study of Level C

Delay

Time difference between target path and reference path

RTT-like method for the target path

Doppler

Angle

AoA at receiver

AoD at transmitter

RSRP/SINR of target path

FFS ambiguity handling of delay/Doppler/angle

FFS how to define a path based on a subset or all delay/Doppler/angle parameters

vivo | Yes | For bistatic sensing, the definitions of delay/range and angle differ from those for monostatic sensing, and measurements related to UE privacy should be considered. The total delay is the sum of Tx–target and target–Rx delays. Separating these two components can enable better localization and privacy protection. The angle differences relative to the LOS path in bistatic plane are more natural for bistatic geometry than absolute AoA/AoD, as they reflect the bistatic geometry and can be measured even when the absolute orientation is unknown. We propose to add the following measurement metrics for further study: Delay/range delay/range of Tx-target delay/range of target-Rx Angle AoD difference in bistatic plane between LOS path and target path at transmitter AoA difference in bistatic plane between LOS path and target path at receiver

A UE can be a CPE, in which case it can know its location very accurately.

LGE | No | We prefer to define Level C measurement based on a scattering point rather than a path. If a path is mainly distinguished by delay as in positioning measurements, ambiguity may remain in Doppler and angle domains. Therefore, point-based measurement is more suitable for representing delay/Doppler/angle-associated sensing results. In addition, NR measurement definitions have been mainly defined based on monostatic and further consideration is needed for bistatic sensing. In particular, the reference point may be different depending on the sensing mode, and therefore its definition should be clarified for monostatic and bistatic sensing. For angle measurement, the expressions “AoA at receiver” and “AoD at transmitter” may be not clear. It is preferred to clarify whether the angle is measured by the receiver or transmitter. For power-related metrics such as sensing SINR, it is also necessary to study how the interference level is defined and measured, since both target and non-target components may affect the SINR measurement. Therefore We suggest modifying the proposal as follows: [FL1][M] Proposal 5.3-1 At least the following measurement metrics of a target point are considered for further study of Level C Delay Time difference between target path and reference point FFS: how to define reference path for monostatic and bistatic sensing RTT-like method for the target point Doppler Angle AoA measured by receiver AoD measured by transmitter RSRP/SINR of target point FFS: How to define interference level Quality/uncertainty/confidence FFS ambiguity handling of delay/Doppler/angle FFS how to define a point based on a subset of interested delay/Doppler/angle parameters

PCL | Yes | Delay, Doppler, and Angle are the essential metrics. For Angle, we specifically support both AoA at the receiver and AoD at the transmitter. This is particularly important for sensing-assisted beam management, as these angular measurements can be directly mapped to beam directions.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
IDCC | Comment | We do not agree with moderator comment that RCS is not valid for level C and only level D, and are concerned by his comment on micro-Doppler being valid but not explicitly included – the work should be contribution driven and moderator should collect and reflect views impartially. Regarding RCS and micro-Doppler, there is a similar relationship to the already included Level-C path metrics. For example, Doppler is already considered as a path-level measurement, while micro-Doppler can be viewed as a finer-granularity Doppler-related characteristic associated with the path. Similarly, RSRP/SINR are already considered as path-level metrics, while RCS can be viewed as a reflection-related characteristic derived from path measurements such as received path power, path loss, and propagation geometry. Therefore, from our perspective, RCS and micro-Doppler can also be measured or estimated for a detected path at Level C, even before final target/object interpretation at Level D. Such sensing-specific characteristics may provide important information for distinguishing target-related paths from surrounding environmental reflections or clutter, and hence benefit the selection or identification of target paths for subsequent Level-D measurements and processing. In addition, while we understand that “at least” does not preclude additional metrics, we believe explicitly mentioning micro-Doppler and RCS would provide clearer study direction for sensing-oriented path characterization in Level-C measurements. We propose the following modification: Doppler/micro-Doppler RSRP/SINR/RCS of target path

Measurement of a scattering point (Level D)

Summary on company views

Level D

Power

RCS: ZTE, Tiami, IDC, Ofinno

A list of RSRPP measured by multiple TRPs: ZTE

RSRPP in 2D RD bin: ZTE

RSRPP in 1D delay bin: ZTE

Confidence metric: CATT

Uncertainty shape + confidence probability: ZTE (TS 23.032)

[Moderator’s note] The definition of Level D is relative clear in 5GA ISAC. The sensing receiver is assumed to have capability to identify a target with single or multiple scattering points. Instead of using multiple options, a general framework could be to define Level D as a method to report measurement results per scattering point. Further, association of measurement across CPI is likely to be a capability especially for a UE.

As to power, I add “if applicable” as the agreement of 5GA ISAC.

Companies are encouraged to provide views on the following proposal.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.4-1

At least the following measurement metrics of a scattering point of a target are considered for further study of Level D

3D or 2D location

3D or 2D velocity

Power if applicable

Uncertainty/confidence

Optionally, association of measurements across CPI

SS: An environmental sensing phase can be configured for a UE to analyze channel and environmental factors (e.g., multipath, blockage, fading, clutter, and interference), improving target detection and identification, with measurements optionally reported to the aNB or SF and processing details left to UE implementation.

[Moderator’s note] Option 1A and 1B are listed for study in the agreement of RAN1 #124bis. Multiple companies provide discussions on the two options. Based on the inputs, the following observation is made

Option 1A

Pros

Low latency of sensing measurement, report and computation

High accuracy by avoiding the loss of information during quantization on sensing data from aNB

Efficient scheduling/control of mutual assistance of sensing and communication

Cons

Privacy concern if certain sensitive information of UE is reported to aNB

Option 1B

Pros

NR LPP can be a reference for the design.

Reporting assistance information of UE to sensing function is often considered as no/less privacy issue

Cons

Larger latency since necessary measurement from UE needs to go to SF first, then go to aNB after SF processing.

Instead of rushing down selection, it is proposed that RAN1 can study RAN1 impacts of both options, since the final choice requires cross-WG coordination and maybe only possible at the end of or after the study item.

For Option 1A (UE-aNB), based on the inputs, it is proposed to study the configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource, and/or measurement report, as well as payload size reduction of the report. The same proposal is made for Option 1B (UE-SF), but the discussion should be limited to issues/solutions from RAN1 perspective.

[FL1][H] Proposal 5.5-1

For UE as sensing receiver, assuming UE measurements is directly sent to aNB (Option 1A), the following aspects can be considered for further study

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement report

Payload size reduction of measurement report

Other aspects are not precluded

[FL1][H] Proposal 5.5-2

For UE as sensing receiver, assuming UE measurements is sent to SF (Option 1B), the following aspects can be considered for further study from RAN1 perspective

Configuration, activation/deactivation and dynamic indication of measurement resource

Payload size reduction of measurement report

Other aspects are not precluded

By splitting it up, it appears as if we have agreed to have separate solutions, or that we understand what “sent directly to anB” means. For some companies “directly” means RRC, but for others it may just mean that there is a higher layer transport protocol with an end-point to the aNB. All these are architecture debates and should not happen in RAN1; they are already happening in RAN2/SA2. Therefore, we support IDCC’s proposal: For UE as sensing receiver, regardless of the reporting endpoint (e.g., assuming UE measurements is sent to aNB or SF) (Option 1B), the following aspects can be considered for further study from RAN1 perspective There is no need to separate the discussion, since the transport of the measurements and/or assistance information should be left up to the other WGs. With regards to the 1A and 1B, both proposals can be merged for now to a potential agreement that says: From RAN1 perspective, UE measurements and/or assistance information can be sent to aNB and/or SF; RAN2 and SA2 to study and decide the transport And we don’t further debate what “directly” means and what it doesn’t mean, without waiting for progress from RAN2/RAN3/SA2.

Or are we studying the necessity to do those? I’d tend to believe the intension is to study the necessity because the ‘how’ issue should be details to be fixed in WI. Then the question is whether they are the issues to study at the beginning of study phase. For example, if we need to study whether to do activation/deactivation and dynamic indication is needed, we should study/discuss the motivation first. At this moment, we don’t know yet or not ready yet to study that aspect because the overall resources needed for sensing is unclear yet. It is unclear yet what the resource will be used for, for detection only, for assisting comm only, or for both? We don’t know yet the reported should be dynamically reported or other ways because we don’t know yet what cases we will discuss it for, for assisting comm, for BM or for CSI, may or may not be the same required reporting behavior. Overall, we still believe such proposals are not helpful for guiding the study.

On the other hand, Option 1B may be suitable for the use case that can tolerate large latency, e.g., environment construction. [FL3][H] Proposal 5.5-1-rev1

For UE as sensing receiver, the proponents should provide motivations for measurement report and assistance information from UE to aNB (Option 1A) and from UE to SF (Option 1B) in RAN1 #126, at least from the following aspects from RAN1 perspective

Latency of sensing resource allocation and of sensing measurement/report

Impact to sensing performance for object detection/tracking

Impact to the communication performance assistance assisted by sensing

The amount of measurement data vs. the maximum payload of L1/L2/high layer report

Privacy of TRP and UE

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
IDCC | Comment | 1- From our perspective, several aspects listed in the proposal appear weakly related or not directly related to the topic of UE measurement/reporting mechanisms themselves. In particular: latency of sensing resource allocation, impact to sensing performance for object detection/tracking, impact to communication performance assisted by sensing, and privacy of TRP, are broader sensing-system or sensing-assisted-communication considerations, rather than issues specific to UE measurement/reporting mechanisms or reporting endpoint selection. For example, sensing performance for object detection/tracking mainly depends on sensing RS design, sensing processing, sensing geometry, sensing algorithms, and sensing deployment assumptions. Similarly, communication-assistance performance depends on the sensing-assisted communication framework and sensing accuracy assumptions, not only on the UE reporting mechanism itself. Therefore, it may be preferable to better scope the proposal toward aspects directly related to UE measurement/reporting behavior and reporting framework impacts from RAN1 perspective. We may also consider adding the following discussion point: Definition of sensing-specific events and event conditions (e.g., detection, tracking, mobility/state changes, threshold crossing, object appearance/disappearance) according to the sensing use case and measurement level (e.g., Level B/C/D), including potential payload-size reduction mechanisms for sensing measurement reports.

[Moderator’s note] It is controversial to report TRP location and/or UE location. So, it is proposed that RAN1 can continue discussions.

The expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity as assistance information were proposed in 5GA ISAC, and it is reported by multiple companies in current SI again. Since it has clear benefit to reduce the SRX processing complexity, the moderator proposes to agree on such assistance information.

Further, due to the limited inputs, no other commonly preferred parameter is found. Therefore, the moderator encourages all interested companies can provides views on necessary assistance information in next meeting.

[FL1][M] Proposal 5.7-1

The information on the expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity of interested target/object can be sent from SF to aNB, from SF to UE, or from aNB to UE

Companies are encouraged to provide input of other assistance information exchanged between SF, aNB and UE in RAN1 #126.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | Assistance information provided by the network (SF/aNB) to Tx/Rx nodes is essential to constrain the sensing search space, thereby reducing latency, power consumption, and implementation complexity. In particular, for bistatic sensing, environmental information such as the LOS path between the Tx and Rx nodes can be utilized as a reference to mitigate time synchronization errors.
ZTE |  | As mentioned by several companies, assistance data such as UE/TRP location, TRP antenna configuration is important for UE/TRP/SF sensing results computation. Hence, we have the following suggestion: Study the assistance information on the expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity of interested target/object sent from SF to aNB, from SF to UE, or from aNB to UE Study the assistance information including UE location sent from UE to TRP or SF, TRP information sent from aNB to SF or UE. Companies are encouraged to provide input of other assistance information exchanged between SF, aNB and UE in RAN1 #126. Note: the coordination with other working groups is needed, e.g. for procedure signaling design, and check UE/TRP privacy authorization, etc.
OPPO | No | The reporting pairing between SF and aNB, between SF and UE, and between aNB and UE are still all study options. But this proposal seems to assume they are all agreed options by saying “can be”. We suggest to make following modification: The information on the expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity of interested target/object can be sent from SF to aNB, from SF to UE, or from aNB to UE to SRX. The SRX is not required to apply the expected ranges in the upcoming sensing measurements.
IDCC | Comment | Further study may be beneficial on target-specific characterization metrics as assistance information exchanged from SF to UE and/or from aNB to UE, such as target type, target characteristics, target mobility behavior, clutter-related information, or sensing-region-related constraints, to further improve sensing processing efficiency and accuracy. TRP/UE location information may also be beneficial as assistance information for improving sensing accuracy, sensing geometry awareness, synchronization, and coordinate transformation between sensing entities. Hence we propose the following modifications: FL1][M] Proposal 5.7-1 The information on the expected range of delay, Doppler, angle, position and/or velocity and/or target specific metrics and/or UE/TRP location of interested target/object can be sent from SF to aNB, from SF to UE, or from aNB to UE Companies are encouraged to provide input of other assistance information exchanged between SF, aNB and UE in RAN1 #126.
vivo | Yes | If UE is an entity for processing the sensing data, TRP location or antenna configuration becomes very important. This is because in general, UE is not capable of estimating the angle, the calculation parameters may only rely on the range of LOS, NLOS, etc. which are less than its necessity. Providing TRP location or antenna configuration can assist to collect all the parameters for sensing estimation.

[Moderator’s note] Based on the contributions, the following observations/suggestions are made by the moderator:

Sensing modes: Some companies show preference to a subset of sensing modes, however, as instructed by RAN, RAN1 should study all 6 sensing modes. Whether a sensing mode is feasible or not should be an outcome of RAN study. Therefore, it is suggested that we keep all 6 sensing modes at the time being. Further, referring to TR 38.765, there is a good motivation for the study of cooperative sensing procedures.

RRC states: Several companies discussed RRC states. However, it is high layer expertise to discuss such issues. Therefore, before any input from high layer, it is suggested that we keep all RRC states open in RAN1 discussions.

Frequency range: it is good to clarify all frequency ranges (FR1/3/2-1) are in scope.

Positioning related: Let’s wait for clear guideline from RAN, e.g., RAN #112

Timing advance: this is one issue for UE as sensing transmitter

AI+sensing: This is a good feature. However, considering basic function of 6G ISAC was not discussed yet and we only have 6 meetings in the 6G study item. It is generally preferred to hold on the related discussions.

Since a concept of cooperative sensing has been used in RAN1 discussions for long time, the moderator proposes to give a definition on it. Companies are encouraged to provide views on the following proposals.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.1-1

Study integration with communication for ISAC in all 6G frequency ranges

Study integration with communication for ISAC using each individual sensing mode

Study integration with communication for ISAC using the combination of multiple sensing modes

[FL1][M] Proposal 6.1-2

Definition: Cooperative sensing is to sense a sensing target with one or multiple sensing modes using multiple sensing transmitters and/or multiple sensing receivers.

[FL3][M] Proposal 6.1-2-rev1

Definition: Cooperative sensing is to sense one or multiple sensing targets with one or multiple sensing modes using multiple sensing transmitters and/or multiple sensing receivers.

Company | Yes or No | Comments
Moderator |  | First proposal is agreed in Monday online session Lets’ continue discuss the second proposal
ETRI | Yes | 
IDCC | Comment | The second proposal may unintentionally limit the interpretation of cooperative sensing to cases involving both multiple sensing transmitters and multiple sensing receivers. However, cooperative sensing may also include cases with a single sensing transmitter and multiple sensing receivers, where cooperation exists across multiple bistatic sensing links/modes in a multi-static sensing operation. Therefore, we propose the following clarification: Definition: Cooperative sensing is to sense one or multiple sensing targets with one or multiple sensing modes using multiple sensing nodes that may include: multiple STX + single SRX, single STX + multiple SRX, or multiple STX + multiple SRX. We are also open to other definition that captures the above in a more eloquent way.

NEC | No | We still don't see the necessity to define cooperative sensing in addition to multi-static sensing. Even in the proposal, cooperative sensing and multi-static sensing are both talking about "using multiple sensing transmitters and/or multiple sensing receivers", we failed to see the difference.

Sensing assisted communication

Summary on company views

Use cases for communication assistance

Power saving: HW, SS, CMCC, CU

UE measurement reduction: HW, SS

avoid unnecessary communication transmission: SS

Sensing assisted mobility/handover: HW, CATT, Apple, SS, CMCC, vivo, Tejas, Wistron, CT, CU

HW: reduce the UE power consumption by 25.4% compared to the full NR measurement

Sensing assisted beam management: HW, Nokia, SS, CMCC, vivo, NIST, ZTE, CATT, Wistron, CT, Panasonic, Tejas, NEC, PCL, CU, Jio, IIT, Sony

Besides communication RSRP

per-path AoA/ZoA or AoD/ZoD: vivo

ZTE: Results on Accurate ratio of communication beam selection and CDF of RSRP gap of estimated communication beams

HW: the sensing assisted scheme can achieve a better beam management performance in terms of beam forming gain with a lower beam sweeping overhead.

Apple: Reactive or predictive

Vivo: Observation 13: Sensing assisted beam management utilizing Type III sensing related information (AoA/ZoA or AoD/ZoD) can achieve up to 97.32% Top-1 beam prediction accuracy with an 87.5% measurement overhead reduction, outperforming pure RSRP-based schemes

Sensing assisted CSI acquisition and CSI feedback: HW, Nokia, SS, CMCC, vivo, Lenovo, NIST, ZTE, CATT, CU, Sony, Tejas

HW: Preliminary results have verified that in scenarios with 256 ports or more, the CSI-RS overhead can be reduced to about one-tenth of the original level with legacy CSI-RS pattern/density design, while the performance loss is less than 3%

Sensing assisted channel estimation: SS, CMCC, vivo

Sensing assisted resource allocation/link adaptation: SS, Tejas, NIST

Control amount of RACH resource for a SSB: SS

Sensing assisted positioning enhancement: CMCC (multipath-assisted positioning) , Lenovo, Panasonic

Event-based communication assistance: Apple

Environment reconstruction: HW, Nokia, SS, CMCC, vivo, Tejas

Not to assume a fully sensing-derived channel reconstruction: SS

Study the useful sensing measurement for communication assistance: HW, SS, vivo, CMCC

delay, angle, power, phase of the detected paths: HW

3GPP or non-3GPP: vivo

3 types of CAI: vivo

Static vs. dynamic path information: HW, CATT

CATT

static measurements: reflector position, AoA/AoD, LoS/NLoS state, and RSRPP.

Dynamic sensing information: velocity/Doppler, trajectory, AoA/AoD variation and blockage indication

Study sensing measurement report: SS

UE reports the measurement to aNB: HW, ZTE

unified channel measurement report (i.e., measurement content/format) for both sensing and communication: HW

aNB obtains UE’ s location information: ZTE, Nokia

Latency analysis

Option 1A (UE-aNB): ZTE (1-3.5ms)

Option 1B (UE-SF-aNB): ZTE (23-58.5ms)

SS: It may be useful for RAN1 to study the following:

what types of sensing information are most relevant for communication assistance

which PHY procedures can benefit from such information

what accuracy and latency are needed for the sensing information to be useful

how the potential gain compares with the additional signaling, reporting, and implementation impact.

Vivo

HW: High-level framework for sensing assisting communication

[Moderator’s note] As captured in TR 38.914, 6G should support communication assistance as a use case for 6G ISAC. Huawei, ZTE, vivo provide evaluation results for sensing assisted mobility, beam management and CSI acquisition. Vivo categorize the measurements to 3 types for communication assistance. Other companies normally give high level discussions on use cases for communication assistance.

In last RAN1 meeting, some companies commented that it is not clear about the use cases for communication assistance. The moderator shares the same view. There were also views that the potential specification impact of communication assistance can be discussed together with object detection/tracking. The moderator thinks it is also a valid point. However, before making such a conclusion, it needs to check for each use case for communication assistance whether additional impacts to physical layer design beyond object detection/tracking is necessary.

In summary, from the moderator’s understanding, RAN1 should progress the discussion on communication assistance from two aspects.

The use cases for communication assistance. Though detailed algorithms are up to implementation choice, but it is good if proponent companies can clarify the full procedure that how sensing results can be used to improve communication performance. Quantitative results and qualitative analysis are both helpful.

The useful sensing measurement for communication assistance. As discussed by multiple companies, the sensing results for communication assistance may be slightly different from that defined for object detection/tracking. One example delay/Doppler/angle of static environment path has large impact on communication performance.

To avoid overlap with 10.8.1, the discussion here is for potential physical layer impacts.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.2-1

The following use cases for sensing assisted communication are proposed by the proponent companies

Sensing assisted beam management

Sensing assisted CSI acquisition and reporting

Sensing assisted mobility/handover

Sensing assisted resource allocation/link adaptation

Sensing assisted channel estimation

Sensing assisted power saving

Sensing assisted positioning enhancement

RAN1 to study the potential impacts if any on physical layer designs for sensing assisted communication beyond what is necessary for object detection/tracking.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | 
OPPO |  | We think the most important thing in RAN1 to study the sensing-assisted communication is to clarify which of the following two options should be taken in RAN1: Option 1: For sensing-assisted communication, what to be sensed is the effectiveness of wireless channel upon the communication. Option 2: For sensing-assisted communication, what to be sensed is the “environmental reason” for the observed channel effectiveness upon the communication. Does the proposal actually suggest Option 1 above?
IDCC | No | We have concerns with the intent and scope of this proposal. This is not suitable as a high-priority AI 10.8.2 proposal. Agreeing to this would turn AI 10.8.2 into an open-ended study of “physical-layer impacts of sensing-assisted communication” before AI 10.8.1 has resolved the evaluation necessity, methodology, metrics/KPIs, or channel-model assumptions. Furthermore, the listed items are procedure-specific communication functions that are still being studied for 6GR and are not yet mature. In addition, how the network eventually uses sensing information to enhance such functions may be implementation-specific. InterDigital supports studying what sensing information may be measured/reported. However, RAN1 should not spend high-priority time at this stage on detailed PHY impacts for beam management, CSI, mobility, scheduling, link adaptation, channel estimation, power saving, or positioning. Wayforward: Defer procedure-specific sensing-assisted communication impacts in AI 10.8.2 until at least AI 10.8.1 has clarified the use cases, evaluation methodology, metrics/KPIs, and channel-model assumptions. AI 10.8.2 should focus instead on sensing measurement/reporting and coexistence aspects.

In ISAC agenda, the procedures including the required sensing information, gains, and potential spec impacts of two or three high-priority use cases need to be clarified.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
Tejas Networks | Okay | We are aligned with the proposal. We suggest prioritizing the listed use cases and focusing the study on a selected set for detailed evaluation.
ETRI | Yes | 
IDCC | No | We still believe it is premature for AI 10.8.2 to study detailed physical-layer impacts of sensing-assisted communication before foundational aspects such as use cases, evaluation methodology, metrics/KPIs, channel-model assumptions, and the evaluation scope of sensing-assisted communication are sufficiently clarified. Furthermore, 6GR communication functions are being studied as we speak and they are not mature for assessing spec impact. AI 10.8.1 is still discussing multiple candidate evaluation scopes for sensing-assisted communication, ranging from: no dedicated communication-assistance evaluation, evaluation of sensing only, evaluation with ideal sensing assistance, evaluation with realistic sensing assistance. Since the evaluation scope itself is not agreed yet, detailed procedure-specific PHY impact studies for sensing-assisted communication in AI 10.8.2 appear premature at this stage. Therefore, AI 10.8.2 should instead focus on sensing measurement/reporting aspects and related coexistence considerations, while procedure-specific PHY impacts for communication functions can be further studied after some of the fundamental enablers are studied/clarified in AI 10.8.1.

Resource allocation/multiplexing

Summary on company views

Multiplexing schemes between sensing and communication:

TDM: Nokia (baseline), NEC (baseline), SS, CATT, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE, NEC (baseline), EURECOM, Sharp, Google, Lenovo, ETRI (baseline), Tiami, Transsion

FDM: Nokia, SS, CATT, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE (subband), Sharp, Lenovo, ETRI, Tiami, IDCC, DCM, Jio

RE level vs. Subband level

CDM: Nokia, SS, CATT, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE, Sharp, Lenovo, ETRI, Tiami, IDCC, DCM, Jio

SDM: Nokia, SS, CATT, Apple, Xiaomi, LGE, Sharp, Lenovo, ETRI, Tiami, IDCC, DCM, Jio

Power domain (OMA): LGE, Cohere, Lenovo

using data channel for sensing: HW, Nokia

measurement gap: Apple, LGE, Sharp, Google

sensing processing window like PPW: CATT

the downlink and uplink time window mechanisms in 5G Rel-18 positioning can be the baseline: CATT

partial-separation resource allocation: Panasonic

multi-stage (detection vs. tracking): Tejas, CT, MTK, CMCC, Apple, AT&T

SSB for initial detection: AT&T

Tracking only RS: CMCC

Multi-resolution sensing: Apple

Support a UE making sensing measurement based on RSs transmitted by a non-serving TRP: E//, QC, Fraunhofer

BWP impacts: Xiaomi, Apple, Sharp, Google, Nokia

allocated in a cell-independent frequency layer: OPPO

allocated within a cell: OPPO

taking into account UE BWP: Nokia

Sensing only BWP: Google

Shared BWP: Google

Collison handling between sensing and communication: CMCC, OPPO, E//, Google

Priority rule: E//, OPPO, CATT, Jio

Sensing RS collides with URLLC, SSB: E//

impact of puncturing on sensing performance: E//

Options to define sensing signal overheads upon communication: OPPO, MTK

[Moderator’s note] Based on the contributions, there is clear interest to study on TDM/FDM/CDM/SDM resource multiplexing schemes between sensing and communication and between sensing signals. Measurement gap is also discussed which requires tight coordination with RAN4. But we can also handle the RAN1 impacts directly. NR Positioning RS is configured in a frequency layer which is independent to BWP for communication, similar issue was discussed by companies for ISAC. Multiple companies propose to study the different sensing RS resources in detection stage and tracking stage.

From moderator’s understanding, collision handling between sensing and communication can be considered as issues of resource multiplexing too.

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
ETRI |  | To ensure that measurement resources are not interfered by other sensing/communication resources, measurement window should be also considered for resource allocation. So, we prefer to modify the last sub-bullet as: - Transmission gap or transmission/measurement window
IDCC | Comments | We believe further clarification is needed regarding the meaning of “transmission gap” and “transmission window” in the proposal. In particular, it is unclear whether “transmission gap” is intended to be equivalent to, overlap with, or differ from conventional “measurement gap”, and whether the definition applies generally to sensing transmitter and/or sensing receiver operation. For example: a measurement gap is typically associated with providing time resources for a UE to perform measurements by temporarily suspending or limiting certain communication activities, while a transmission gap may additionally imply transmission muting, interference reduction, sensing reception opportunity, or Tx/Rx switching behavior. Similarly, clarification is needed regarding the meaning of “transmission window” and its relation to CPI. For example, it is unclear whether: a transmission window corresponds to a full CPI, a portion/sub-interval of a CPI, or a scheduling/allocation duration independent of CPI definition. Therefore, we suggest clarifying the intended meaning and scope of “transmission gap” and “transmission window” before further discussion on related resource allocation mechanisms. 2- It may also be beneficial to clarify whether these aspects are intended to be studied from a RAN1 waveform/resource allocation perspective or from a broader system/procedural perspective.
Ericssoncomment |  | From our understanding, tx gap accounts for the retuning needs when sensing and communication are in some way not aligned (e.g bandwidth, carrier, power settings are different between the two). For transmission window, we should also add a definition. We understand it as a exclusive resource allocation to sensing, e.g. all other resources that were potentially scheduled according to configuration are dropped in favor of sensing.
ZTE |  | We are happy to study bandwidth and measurement gap. But maybe not only bandwidth should be focused on. From my understanding, position of allocated band also matters. To be simplified, we believe to directly say frequency domain resource allocation would be better for first bullet. Proposal For study on resource allocation within sensing and between sensing and communication, consider at least the following aspects. resource allocation for sensing transmission resource that may be different from bandwidth of resource allocation for communication Transmission gap or transmission window
ITRI | Yes | 
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Agreeing on something unclear is not helpful for further study. So let’s clarify what the transmission gap means. What the transmission window means How these should be considered for the later on study.
Moderator | Thanks ZTE, revised the first sub-bullet, hope it is better now It seems the definition of transmission gap, transmission window, measurement gap, measurement window is not clear. Just share some observations based on studies. Transmission gap means a period that STX cannot transmit, which can be due to BWP switching of the STX or before after the transmission gap of the STX. Therefore, transmission gap must be coupled with other features. The “other feature” is more important, so we don’t need to emphasize transmission gap too much Transmission window is the time window for STX transmission, which belongs to a part of the behavior of measurement gap or measurement window. I mean, in a measurement gap or a measurement window, STX to transmission (so measurement gap or a measurement window behavior like a transmission window) SRX does sensing measurement In summary, it seems “measurement gap and/or a measurement window” are better to cover the intended behaviors. | Thanks ZTE, revised the first sub-bullet, hope it is better now It seems the definition of transmission gap, transmission window, measurement gap, measurement window is not clear. Just share some observations based on studies. Transmission gap means a period that STX cannot transmit, which can be due to BWP switching of the STX or before after the transmission gap of the STX. Therefore, transmission gap must be coupled with other features. The “other feature” is more important, so we don’t need to emphasize transmission gap too much Transmission window is the time window for STX transmission, which belongs to a part of the behavior of measurement gap or measurement window. I mean, in a measurement gap or a measurement window, STX to transmission (so measurement gap or a measurement window behavior like a transmission window) SRX does sensing measurement In summary, it seems “measurement gap and/or a measurement window” are better to cover the intended behaviors.

Phase continuity

Summary on company views

Study phase continuity within CPI: HW, QC, MTK, Apple, ZTE, Xiaomi, CATT, Tejas, SRPD, SS, IDCC, Apple, Sharp, vivo, Jio, LGE, Honor, Cohere, CT

TA: ZTE, CATT, SPRD, Tejas, ZTE, IDCC

NO: HW

Consider both timing advance command (TAC) and automatic timing adjustment (ATA): CATT

transmitting power: OPPO, Apple, ZTE, E//, Xiaomi, CATT, IDCC, Tejas, SPRD

NO: HW

TCI state: ZTE, Tejas, Jio, IDCC

beam switching: QC

UL/DL switches for TDD system: HW, CATT, SPRD, Tejas, IDCC, Sharp, vivo, ZTE

oscillator drift: QC

BWP switching: SPRD, Jio

Whether/how to recover phase continuity?

Drop interrupted measurements: ZTE

Recover phase continuity: CATT

Reference path: HW, ZTE, E//, QC

LOS ray: ZTE

Ray from sensing reference object: ZTE

RS: HW, Xiaomi, Honor

frequency-domain overlapping resources: HW, vivo

define the sensing phase continuity capability for UEs and TRPs: CATT

Zak-OTFS: Cohere

[Moderator’s note] Many companies discussion phase continuity issue, which is hence considered as a critical issue for 6G ISAC. Without phase continuity, Doppler cannot be estimated which breaks the basis for ISAC operation. HW report a result showing that a single event for phase discontinuity, e.g., TA change or power change within a CPI only have marginal impacts to Doppler estimation. However, some other events, e.g., TDD DL/UL switch may happen frequently in a CPI which requires special handling. More evaluations would be helpful to move forward.

Companies are encouraged to provide views on the following proposal.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.4-1

On phase continuity for DL/UL sensing RS, study at least the following aspects.

Factors that impact phase continuity in a CPI, e.g., TDD UL/DL switch, beam switching, frequency hopping, change of power/TA, Tx/Rx chain reconfiguration, etc.

How to maintain phase continuity in a CPI?

Whether/how to recover phase continuity in a CPI?

Other aspects are not precluded

Companies are encouraged to bring up evaluation results on phase continuity issues in RAN1 #126.

study overhead reduction for beam sweeping for bistatic: Nokia

Tx and Rx beam sweeping in DL RS configuration: E//

study and support detection and tracking scheme in sensing beam management: CMCC

Beam-related measurement metrics: CATT, vivo, IDC, CT

RSRPP or SINR per target path: CATT

Beam report: SS, IDCC, CATT

beam-aware reporting: SS

Tx and Rx beam sweeping in measurement reporting: E//

Beam indication: SS, CATT, IDC, SPRD

Beam maintenance and failure recovery: IDC, SPRD, DCM, NEC

Digital precoder: IDC, Apple, Fraunhofer

Study precoding and reporting mechanisms for sensing, e.g., eType2 CSI: Fraunhofer

beam-related assistance information: SS

UL sensing RS

reuse SRS resources/resource sets and beam mechanism from communication SRS: E//

E//: Support Tx and Rx beam sweeping in DL RS configuration and measurement reporting

Apple: Study the precoding/beamforming mechanisms needed to accommodate both communications and sensing requirements.

[Moderator’s note] Many contributions discuss beam management for ISAC which is motivated by distinct features of sensing compared with communication.

The valid sensing beam pair is likely to be different from the best communication beam pair

Due to the extremely weak received power of target path at the SRX, it is expected that sensing performance is more vulnerable to interference which motivates strict beam coordination than communication.

The metric for communication beam management is the RSRP/SINR of whole received signals at receiver, while the sensing performance is mainly determined by power of a target path and the interference nearby, i.e., RSRP/SINR per target path

Further, the target paths (STX-ST-SRX) may arrive at receiver from multiple directions, which motivates the report of Rx beam related information.

All/most proposed issues have the correspondence in communication beam management. To adapt the sensing specific issues, it is expected some changes/updates to communication beam management are needed. On the other hand, as proposed by some companies, a unified beam management framework for communication and sensing may be preferred, if applicable. Above all, communication beam management can be the starting point for sensing specific discussions.

Beam indication

Beam maintenance and failure recovery

Similar as communication beam management, we are feeling sensing RS beam management, e.g. TCI, RS resource may be just a part of sensing RS configuration. Also, for beam related reporting, we may not need a dedicated beam reporting for sensing, in other words, the beam related indication such as beam index, RSRPP/path reporting can be just a part of sensing measurement report. Hence, we suggest the following two notes to clarify the understanding at least from spec impact perspective. On beam management for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, taking communication beam management as starting point. IDCC | Yes | 
vivo | Yes | For dedicated sensing RS, sensing-oriented BM is needed. For “reuse communication RS and enhanced communication RS” for sensing but not used for communication, sensing-oriented BM is needed. For “reuse communication RS and enhanced communication RS” for sensing and also is used for communication, joint communication and sensing-oriented BM is needed.
SONY | Yes | 
Xiaomi | [yes] | Yes, in principle. Some clarification may be needed: For “beam indication”, what is the difference compared to TCI states? For example, is this intended to support RX beam indication from UE to aNB? What is meant with “Beam maintenance” in the context of sensing? In communication, we usually mean that the UE “maintains” its best RX beam for given TX beams (best TX/RX beam pair). The UE measures continuously SSB and maintains its best RX beams for each SSB. Then, when it gets a TCI state indicated for a data transmission, it can pick the best RX beam according to the QCL:ed SSB. However, for sensing, this is different. At least for initial detection. There, the UE probably has to sweep its RX beams for the same TX beam (similar to P3 stage), the scan a large angular area.
Qualcomm | No | We consider this as low priority; As a first thought, communication-based beam management are applicable to sensing, and no specific handling may be needed for sensing. We acknowledge that there could be specific enhancenemtns one could envision, but given the current progress, and the open items that exist, we consider this as lower priority.

LGE | Yes | We support studying sensing-oriented beam management, considering differences from communication. In particular, beam alignment should consider the target sensing area (TSA), where sensing Tx/Rx beams are directed toward the TSA rather than communication-centric directions. In addition, beam design and selection should consider path-level observability, i.e., the ability to resolve and distinguish target-related paths from non-target/background paths.

Interference handling

Summary on company views

Between sensing and communication: SS, DCM, CATT, CMCC, LGE, ETRI, ZTE

To avoid 3dB increase of noise floor, communication within 5km should be muted: CMCC

Between sensing signals: SS, DCM, CATT, LGE, ETRI, ZTE

Increased LOS interference between cells for low-altitude use case: CATT

BS-BS interference: ZTE

UE-UE interference: ZTE

Self-interference for monostatic sensing mode

Up to implemenation: DCM

Large antenna isolation: ZTE

LFM: ZTE

Pulse: ZTE

Interference handling solutions: Apple, ETRI, OPPO, IDC, SS, CATT, ZTE, Huawei

Coordinated resource allocation: Google

Transparent to communicaiton UE or sensing-incapable UE: OPPO

“none” type resources in AI 10.8.3: E//

[Moderator’s note] based on the contributions, it is common view that certain interference handling between sensing signals and between sensing and communication is an important topic for study. Various solutions are also general discussed by different companies, including the interference mitigation/coordination in time/frequency domain, spatial domain or power domain, etc. Please comment on the following proposal.

Power control

Summary on company views

Study power control for sensing: DCM, CATT, OPPO, Apple, NEC, LGE, ZTE, Nokia, vivo, SPRD, Cohere, IDCC, CAICT, Google

NO: HW

Consider both sensing performance and impacts to communication: DCM, vivo, Xiaomi

Two ISAC approaches of communication/sensing common signals/resources and sensing dedicated signals/resources: DCM

Constant power in a CPI: OPPO, Apple, E//, Xiaomi

Power control for shared RS for communication and sensing: LGE

power boosting of the sensing RS: LGE

collaborative design for power-related procedures between sensing and communication: NEC

UL PC: NokiaSPRD, vivo, OPPO, ZTE, CAICT, Xiaomi

P0/alpha: Xiaomi, E//, Apple, IDCC

Separate OL PC on sensing RS aiming at reference path and target path: E//

Pathloss reference

pathloss of STX-target-SRX path: CATT, LGE, NEC, ZTE, Xiaomi, Apple, vivo, NEC

control sensing signal Tx power based on the distance between the TSA and the sensing Tx and/or Rx: LGE

sensing reference entity: Apple

Apple: Multiple SRX experiences different coupling loss to the target, requiring multiple power reference points

OL vs. CL

OL PC as starting point: E//

At least OL PC: CATT

CL PC: Xiaomi, Apple, CAICT, IDCC, CATT

one or more sensing power control parameter sets (P0, alpha, closed-loop index): Apple

Sensing PHR procedure: NEC, Apple, IDCC

power ramping mechanisms: Apple

CATT: two solutions independent or dependent on DL RS measurement

DL PC: Nokia, ZTE, IDCC

Downlink power allocation for 5G communication can be used as a starting point: CATT

Dedicated power offset on Sensing RS, which is independent with that of communication: ZTE

[Moderator’s note] More companies propose to study power control for both UL sensing and DL sensing. Sensing performance and impact to communication are two main key consideration points. A key observation is that sensing performance is determined by the target path, but not the full received power arrived at UE, which may make it different from communication. As discussed by Apple, due to the broadcast nature of sensing RS, the Tx power control should consider the reception of multiple SRX. Some companies propose constant power in a CPI which is actually related to phase continuity in section 6.4.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1

On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication.

PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC)

P0/alpha

Pathloss reference

CL PC

PHR

Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
CATT, CICTCI | Yes | Regarding UL PC, it is necessary to study how to compensate the pathloss of STX-target-SRX path, rather than STX-SRX, At least open-loop power control should be supported. For DL power allocation, we remain open to further discussion. The Sensing RS power offset could be configured either independently of or dependent on the communication signals.
ZTE |  | Again, we prefer to clarify this is for sensing RS, rather than data. We don’t think additional PC is needed in the case data is an sensing signal. On power control for sensing RS, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)
IDCC | Yes | The current proposal explicitly mentions CL PC under UL power control aspects, which may implicitly narrow the study scope toward closed-loop-only approaches. However, sensing operation may involve diverse deployment scenarios, sensing latency constraints, mobility conditions, and signaling overhead considerations where open-loop power control (OL PC) mechanisms may also be beneficial. Therefore, the study should preserve flexibility to evaluate both open-loop and closed-loop power control approaches for sensing signals from UE. Hence, we propose the following modifications: [FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1 On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC OL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)
vivo | Yes | For SRS for both sensing and communication, joint PC is needed. For SRS only used for sensing, sensing-oriented PC is needed.
Xiaomi | Yes | 
Qualcomm | No | We consider this as low priority; As a first thought, communication-based power control are applicable to sensing, and no specific handling may be needed for sensing. We acknowledge that there could be specific enhancements one could envision, but given the current progress, and the open items that exist, we consider this as lower priority.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Same proposal as the last meeting and we made the comments already in the last meeting. We also studied this issue after the last meeting but we still suspect the necessity for the specific power control handling for sensing. The reason is the communication and sensing share the same PA hardware, simply adjusting the PA to satisfy the STx-ST-SRx path may not be feasible at all if the PA has maximized the transmit power to satisfy the STx-SRx path for communication. On the other hand, if sensing is to assist comm, it may even not be expected to have different transmit power from that for comm. Again, such details are not helpful for guiding the study. Instead, the more general proposal can go like Study the necessity of specific power control for sensing for aNB-UE bistatic sensing compared to the power control mechanism for communication.
Nokia | No | At first we should study under what conditions that sensing PC is needed. Without clear justifications, it’s hard to design these PC parameters.
MediaTek | NO | It’s a little bit early to discuss the power control related aspects since there is not any details design on sensing RS/resource allocation aspects, it can be discussed at WI phase.
LGE |  | We support studying sensing-specific power control mechanisms. Power control should consider target-path characteristics rather than conventional link-based metrics, and balance sensing performance with communication impact. In addition, we suggest to study power boosting if sensing RS is RE-level FDMed with communication channel/signal to enhance detectability of weak target reflections. [FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1 On power control for ISAC, study at least the following aspects, considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation) Power boosting for sensing RS
Apple | Yes | There is a need to add a dynamic power control or power ramping method similar to PRACH.
NEC | Yes | PC and PHR is important from PHY procedure to ensure a high-quality sensing.
Samsung |  | We are generally fine with this proposal. In our view, PC for sensing is to determine the appropriate power for target detection/tracking while the PC for communication is to maintain the reliable UE-TRP communication link. Considering the different purposes, we think it is reasonable to study OL/CL PC at least for UL sensing.
PCL |  | We see the value of this topic. However, detailed power control parameters might be premature until the foundational aspects (RS design, beam management, evaluation methodology) are more mature. Our suggestion would be to defer this to a later stage (e.g., RAN1 #126bis or #127) to allow for a more productive discussion.
Moderator |  | Considering inputs from QC, HW, MTK, Nokia, I add “necessity” in the proposal. So, please interested company clarify the motivation and solutions when you discuss PC in future discussions.
Huawei, HiSilicon | Comment | Same proposal as the last meeting and we made the comments already in the last meeting. We also studied this issue after the last meeting but we still suspect the necessity for the specific power control handling for sensing. The reason is the communication and sensing share the same PA hardware, simply adjusting the PA to satisfy the STx-ST-SRx path may not be feasible at all if the PA has maximized the transmit power to satisfy the STx-SRx path for communication. On the other hand, if sensing is to assist comm, it may even not be expected to have different transmit power from that for comm. Again, such details are not helpful for guiding the study. Instead, the more general proposal can go like Study the necessity of specific power control for sensing for aNB-UE bistatic sensing compared to the power control mechanism for communication.

[FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1-rev1

Study the necessity and related specification impacts of power control for ISAC considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication.

PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC)

P0/alpha

Pathloss reference

CL PC

PHR

Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)

[TABLE]
Company | Yes or No | Comments
IDCC | Comment | We still believe OL PC should remain under study for ISAC, since sensing operation may involve diverse deployment scenarios, sensing latency constraints, mobility conditions, and signaling overhead considerations where OL PC may be beneficial. Therefore, explicitly including only CL PC may unintentionally narrow the study scope toward closed-loop-only approaches. Hence, we propose the following modification: [FL1][H] Proposal 6.7-1-rev1 Study the necessity and related specification impacts of power control for ISAC considering both sensing performance and impacts to communication. PC of sensing signals from UE (a.k.a., UL PC) P0/alpha Pathloss reference CL PC OL PC PHR Power allocation of sensing signals from TRP (a.k.a., DL power allocation)
Modetator |  | I think P0/alpha and Pathloss reference are both for OL PC. Maybe I should write down TPC instead of CL PC. Another way is to only list OL PC and CL PC. However, I prefer the first way for more detailed information.

NEC | Yes | Yes | We support. We support PHR study especially for UE UL power control, it's a important component of PC. Surely, current PHR is considered for SCH, but we are studying 6G ISAC, we should allow new procedures /features that benefits ISAC. PHR is an important procedure to ensure the awareness of UE's transmission power statues at serving cell (serving BS), and can also benefit sensing signal transmission at UE based on power-awareness at the serving cell or corresponding SenF.
